# Monitoring - Full Documentation

# Getting Alerts (Canada and Japan)

> For the full documentation index, see: https://docs.mulesoft.com/llms.txt

**Open Beta Release**: In the Canada and Japan Clouds, Logs and Alerts features are in open beta.
Any use of Anypoint Monitoring features in the beta state is subject to the applicable beta services
terms and conditions.

<table><colgroup><col></colgroup><tbody><tr><td><p><span><img src="_images/info.png" alt="info" width="15" height="15" title="This feature requires an Advanced subscription in a supported Anypoint Platform region."></span> Availability: <a href="./#billing">Advanced tier only</a></p></td></tr></tbody></table>

In the [Canada](https://ca1.platform.mulesoft.com/monitoring) and
[Japan](https://jp1.platform.mulesoft.com/monitoring) Clouds, create and receive alert notifications
for your deployed resources (Mule app and APIs).

![Alerts UI](_images/alerts-hyperforce.png)

<table><tbody><tr><td><i data-value="1"></i><b>1</b></td><td>Alerts in Anypoint Monitoring</td></tr><tr><td><i data-value="2"></i><b>2</b></td><td><a href="#alert-properties">Alert properties</a></td></tr><tr><td><i data-value="3"></i><b>3</b></td><td>List of configured alerts.<div><p>Each alert name opens to more information about the alert, including the alert threshold and recipients of alert notifications:</p></div><div><div><img src="_images/alerts-hyperforce-popup.png" alt="Alert details" width="40%"></div></div><div><ol type="a"><li><p>Name of the selected alert</p></li><li><p><a href="#threshold">Threshold configuration</a> for triggering the alert</p></li><li><p>Email address that receives an alert notification when the alert triggers</p></li></ol></div></td></tr><tr><td><i data-value="4"></i><b>4</b></td><td><strong>Create Alert</strong> is for <a href="#create">configuring a new alert</a>.</td></tr><tr><td><i data-value="5"></i><b>5</b></td><td>Enable, disable, or delete an alert from its <span><img src="_images/more-menu-vert-ellipsis.png" alt="" width="15" height="15"></span> (<strong>More</strong> menu).</td></tr></tbody></table>

For alerts in other Anypoint Platform clouds, see [Getting Alerts (US and EU Clouds)](alerts-us-eu).

## Before You Begin

Required prerequisites:

- Your organization must subscribe to a [tier](./#billing) in an Anypoint Platform cloud that
  supports this feature.
- You must have the [permissions](am-permissions) required for viewing or managing Anypoint
  Monitoring.

- At least one Mule application or API must be deployed to a supported host:
  - Mule application:
    - CloudHub 2.0
    - Runtime Fabric
    - Hybrid

  - API managed by Mule Gateway or Omni Gateway (formerly Flex Gateway)

    See [Anypoint Platform Gateways Overview](../gateway-home/).

For information about deployment targets for Mule applications, see
[Runtime Plane Hosting Options](../hosting-home/#runtime-plane-hosting-options).

## Find Alerts

To find the list of alerts:

1.  In Anypoint Monitoring, navigate to **Alerts**.
2.  Review the alert list that returns.

    Each alert provides:
    - **Alert Name**: Name of the alert
    - **Severity**: Configured [severity](#severity) of the alert
    - **Status**: Current [status](#status) of the alert
    - **Metric**: Name of the [metric](#metrics) that the alert reports
    - **Last Alerted**: Most recent date and time the alert triggered or **\-** for an alert that
      has never triggered
    - **Creation Date**: Alert creation date and time
    - **Availability**: **Enabled** or **Disabled** alert setting

## Create an Alert

Configure an alert, the condition for triggering the alert, and the alert notification.

1.  From **Alerts** in Anypoint Monitoring, click **Create Alert**.
2.  Configure alert properties:
    1.  Provide a name for the alert.
    2.  Select the [environment](#environment) in your organization for this alert.
    3.  Select the resource type and resource that the alert is for. Only active resources that can
        receive traffic are available.
        - **Resource Type**: **API** or **Mule Application**
        - **Resource:** Name of the API or Mule application

    4.  For an API alert, select an API version and up to ten response codes.
    5.  Click **Next** to open **Set Alert Condition**.

3.  In **Set Alert Condition**, configure the criteria for triggering the alert:
    1.  Select the [metric](#metrics) that the alert uses.
    2.  Select the [severity](#severity) of the alert.
    3.  Provide a [threshold](#threshold) for triggering the alert.
    4.  Click **Next** to open **Configure Email Notification**.

4.  In **Configure Email Notification**, configure the email to send when the alert triggers.

    To use variables in the email, see [Email Notification Variables](#variables).
    1.  Provide a subject.
    2.  Provide the message.
    3.  Identify recipients.

## Enable, Disable, or Delete an Alert

Alerts are enabled by default when they are created. You can disable, re-enable, or delete an alert.

1.  In Anypoint Monitoring, navigate to **Alerts**.
2.  Find the alert in the Alerts table.
3.  Click the alertâs ![](_images/more-menu-vert-ellipsis.png) (**More** menu).
4.  Enable, disable, or delete the alert.

## Alert Statuses

The **Alerts** page indicates whether an alert is currently triggering or in some other state.

No data

No data is available in the series to evaluate the alert threshold.

Paused

The alert is disabled and no longer active.

Alerting

The value of the metric passes the specified threshold.

OK

The value of the metric has not passed the threshold.

Pending

The alert is enabled and waiting for evaluation.

## Alert Severities

Use your organizationâs alerting criteria when setting an alertâs severity. Available severity
levels are:

- **Low**
- **Medium**
- **High**
- **Critical**

## Alert Thresholds

Alert threshold configurations indicate when the data collected on a [metric](#metrics) triggers the
alert. Configurations for alert thresholds follow this form:

<table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Configuration Pattern</th><th>Example</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><div><div><p><strong>Trigger alert when selected metric is <em>Above|Less than|Above or equal to|Less than or equal to|Equal to</em> <em>Number</em> for each of the last <em>Number</em> consecutive minutes.</strong></p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p><strong>Trigger alert when selected metric is <em>Above</em> <em>4</em> for each of the last <em>10</em> consecutive minutes.</strong></p></div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

The example configures an alert to trigger when the selected metric (such as **Message Count**) is
**Above** 4 for 10 consecutive minutes. In other words, the count must exceed 4 for each minute of
minutes 1-10. If the count exceeds 4 for 9 of 10 minutes, but not the tenth minute, the alert
doesnât trigger, even if the count exceeds 4 at minute 11. Following the same criteria, if the count
exceeds 4 for each minute of minutes 11-20, the alert triggers.

## Alert Metrics

A Mule application or API metric is part of the
[configuration for triggering the alert](#threshold).

Mule app metrics:

- **Message Count**: Total number of Mule messages that processed successfully
- **Message Error Count**: Number of Mule messages that return an exception in a flow
- **Average Response Time**: Response time (in milliseconds) of a message
- **CPU Utilization**: Percent of CPU used on the virtual machine

  This metric includes all processes and is available only for apps deployed to CloudHub or Anypoint
  Runtime Fabric.

- **Memory Utilization**: Percent of heap used

  This metric is available only for apps deployed to CloudHub or Anypoint Runtime Fabric.

  Anypoint Monitoring tracks heap usage and triggers memory alerts based on the value of
  `heap_used`/`heap_total`. Note that `heap_total` isnât identical to the maximum heap memory
  allocated to the Mule app on CloudHub. For example, 0.2 vCores indicates that the maximum heap
  memory is 1 GB (see the table in
  [CloudHub Workers](../cloudhub/cloudhub-architecture#cloudhub-workers)), but the value of
  `heap_total` isnât 1GB. Instead, `heap_total` is the maximum amount of memory that the JVM can use
  for heap memory, where objects are created and stored. In Java, this value changes dynamically
  depending on memory fragmentation, garbage collection, and other memory management features.

- **Thread Count**: Total number of threads used

  This metric is available only for apps deployed to CloudHub or Anypoint Runtime Fabric.

API metrics:

- **Total Request Count**: Sum of requests in the selected time period for the given API

  Data is aggregated in one minute increments.

- **Average Response Time**: Average response time of requests in the selected time period for the
  given API

  Data is aggregated in one minute increments.

- **Response Codes**: Sum of HTTP response codes that occur in the selected time period

## Environments

Alerts are specific to one of your Anypoint Platform environments.

Your organization administrator
[creates](../access-management/environments#to-create-a-new-environment) the environments that are
available to you.

Anypoint Platform supports these types of
[environments](../access-management/environments#types-of-environments.adoc):

- **Design**
- **Production**
- **Sandbox**

## Email Notification Variables

Alerts deliver notifications as emails to designated recipients for each alert. Your email template
can incorporate variables in the **Subject** and **Message** fields. For the description of each
variable, see [Variable Definitions](#alert-variables).

<table><caption>Table 1. Variables for Alerts</caption> <colgroup><col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Alert Type</th><th>Metric</th><th>Applicable Variables</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td rowspan="6"><div><div><p>CloudHub App Alerts</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p><strong>CPU Utilization</strong></p></div></div></td><td rowspan="6"><div><div><ul><li><p><code>${severity}</code></p></li><li><p><code>${operator}</code></p></li><li><p><code>${threshold}</code></p></li><li><p><code>${period}</code></p></li><li><p><code>${value}</code></p></li><li><p><code>${condition}</code></p></li><li><p><code>${organization}</code></p></li><li><p><code>${environment}</code></p></li><li><p><code>${alertLink}</code></p></li><li><p><code>${dashboardLink}</code></p></li><li><p><code>${timestamp}</code></p></li><li><p><code>${resource}</code></p></li></ul></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><div><div><p><strong>Memory Utilization</strong></p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><div><div><p><strong>Thread Count</strong></p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><div><div><p><strong>Message Count</strong></p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><div><div><p><strong>Message Error Count</strong></p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><div><div><p><strong>Message Response Time</strong></p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td rowspan="6"><div><div><p>Anypoint Runtime Fabric App Alerts</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p><strong>CPU Utilization</strong></p></div></div></td><td rowspan="6"><div><div><ul><li><p><code>${severity}</code></p></li><li><p><code>${operator}</code></p></li><li><p><code>${threshold}</code></p></li><li><p><code>${period}</code></p></li><li><p><code>${value}</code></p></li><li><p><code>${condition}</code></p></li><li><p><code>${organization}</code></p></li><li><p><code>${environment}</code></p></li><li><p><code>${alertLink}</code></p></li><li><p><code>${dashboardLink}</code></p></li><li><p><code>${timestamp}</code></p></li><li><p><code>${resource}</code></p></li></ul></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><div><div><p><strong>Memory Utilization</strong></p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><div><div><p><strong>Thread Count</strong></p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><div><div><p><strong>Message Count</strong></p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><div><div><p><strong>Message Error Count</strong></p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><div><div><p><strong>Message Response Time</strong></p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td rowspan="6"><div><div><p>API Alerts</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p><strong>Total Request Count</strong></p></div></div></td><td rowspan="4"><div><div><ul><li><p><code>${severity}</code></p></li><li><p><code>${operator}</code></p></li><li><p><code>${threshold}</code></p></li><li><p><code>${period}</code></p></li><li><p><code>${value}</code></p></li><li><p><code>${condition}</code></p></li><li><p><code>${organization}</code></p></li><li><p><code>${environment}</code></p></li><li><p><code>${alertLink}</code></p></li><li><p><code>${dashboardLink}</code></p></li><li><p><code>${timestamp}</code></p></li><li><p><code>${api}</code></p></li><li><p><code>${policy}</code></p></li></ul></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><div><div><p><strong>Average Response Time</strong></p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><div><div><p><strong>Response Codes</strong></p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><div><div><p><strong>Policy Violations</strong></p></div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

<table id="alert-variables"><caption>Table 2. Variable Definitions</caption> <colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Variable</th><th>Definition</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><code>${alertLink}</code></p></td><td><p>URL of the triggered alert</p></td></tr><tr><td><p><code>${alertState}</code></p></td><td><p>State of the alert when it was triggered.</p><p>Available values are <code>OK</code> or <code>Alerting</code>.</p></td></tr><tr><td><p><code>${api}</code></p></td><td><p>Name of the API that triggers the alert</p></td></tr><tr><td><p><code>${comparison}</code></p></td><td><p>Above, above or equal, equal, below or equal, below</p></td></tr><tr><td><p><code>${condition}</code></p></td><td><p>Metric for which the alert is triggered</p></td></tr><tr><td><p><code>${dashboardLink}</code></p></td><td><p>URL that points to the built-in dashboard for the app or API that triggers the alert</p></td></tr><tr><td><p><code>${environment}</code></p></td><td><p>Name of the environment in which the resource is deployed</p></td></tr><tr><td><p><code>${memberName}</code></p></td><td><p>Name of the server within the server group or cluster</p></td></tr><tr><td><p><code>${operator}</code></p></td><td><p>An operator, such as <code>&lt; &gt; =</code></p></td></tr><tr><td><p><code>${organization}</code></p></td><td><p>Name of the organization that owns the alert</p></td></tr><tr><td><p><code>${period}</code></p></td><td><p>Period of time over which to measure</p></td></tr><tr><td><p><code>${policy}</code></p></td><td><p>Name of the policy whose violation triggers the alert</p></td></tr><tr><td><p><code>${resource}</code></p></td><td><div><div><p>Name of the item that triggers the alert:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>CloudHub Apps: Name of the app that triggers the alert</p></li><li><p>Hybrid Apps: Name of the resource in the app that triggers the alert</p></li></ul></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><code>${severity}</code></p></td><td><p>Severity of the alert</p></td></tr><tr><td><p><code>${targetName}</code></p></td><td><p>Name of the server, server group, or cluster</p></td></tr><tr><td><p><code>${targetState}</code></p></td><td><p>Server state when the alert was triggered</p></td></tr><tr><td><p><code>${threshold}</code></p></td><td><p>Count over the period of time that triggers the alert</p></td></tr><tr><td><p><code>${timestamp}</code></p></td><td><p>Time when the alert was triggered</p></td></tr><tr><td><p><code>${value}</code></p></td><td><p>Value of the metric that triggers the alert</p></td></tr></tbody></table>
---

# Getting Alerts (US and EU Clouds)

> For the full documentation index, see: https://docs.mulesoft.com/llms.txt

[![](../_/img/icons/info.svg)](/monitoring/#billing)

Alerts indicate whether a resource (such as a Mule app or API) is behaving as expected or exceeding
a defined threshold.

![Alerts in Anypoint Monitoring](_images/intro-alerts.png)

In the [US](https://anypoint.mulesoft.com/monitoring) and
[EU](https://eu1.anypoint.mulesoft.com/monitoring) Clouds, you can create these types of alerts from
Anypoint Monitoring:

- [Basic alerts](basic-alerts) for servers, Mule apps, and APIs
- [Custom dashboard alerts](custom-dashboard-alerts) for graphs in custom dashboards in Anypoint
  Monitoring
- [Other alerts in API Manager or Runtime Manager](#other-alerts):
  - Operational alerts for APIs through API Manager
  - Operational alerts for server and app events through Runtime Manager

For alerts in Anypoint Monitoring, you can:

- Enable or disable alerts configured for graphs in custom dashboards
- Filter by [alert status](#alert_status)
- Filter by [alert severity](#alert_severity)

## Alert Thresholds

For basic and custom dashboard alerts in Anypoint Monitoring, thresholds are checked every five
minutes. The evaluated query is the average value of the metric in the last five minutes. For custom
dashboard alerts, the evaluated metric is the one referenced in the **General** tab for the graph.
If the value of the metric passes the threshold, the alert triggers. The alert triggers only if the
alert state changes (from `OK` state to `Alerting` state or vice versa). If you change the email
address of the alert recipient, the recipient doesnât receive an email until the alert triggers.

## Alert Limits

The number of active alerts allocated to your organization depends on your organizationâs pricing
model.

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Pricing Package</th><th>Basic Alerts</th><th>Advanced Alerts</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Anypoint Integration Advanced</p></td><td><p>10 per app or API instance</p></td><td><p>20 across the organization</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Gold Subscription</p></td><td><p>50 across the organization</p></td><td><p>None</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Platinum Subscription</p></td><td><p>50 across the organization</p></td><td><p>None</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Titanium Subscription</p></td><td><p>50 times the number of vCores in your org, plus 100</p></td><td><p>20 across the organization</p></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Filter Alerts by Alert State

Filter alerts based on state using the following filters:

All states

Lists all alerts

No data

No data is available in the series to evaluate the alert threshold.

Paused

The alert is disabled and no longer active.

Alerting

The value of the metric passes the specified threshold.

OK

The value of the metric has not passed the threshold.

Pending

The alert is enabled and waiting for evaluation.

### Filter Alerts by Alert Severity

When you create basic and custom dashboard alerts, you can select the severity for those alerts. The
severity indicates how important the alert is for the recipients of the alerts.

Filter alerts based on severity using the following filters:

All severities

Lists all severities

Not defined

Lists alerts that do not have severities assigned

Info

May be assigned to alerts that do not require immediate attention when triggered. This severity
indicates the metric should be passively monitored.

Warning

May be assigned to alerts that require prompt attention when triggered. This severity indicates an
alert should be closely monitored.

Critical

May be assigned to alerts that require immediate attention when triggered. This severity indicates
an alert should receive an immediate response.

### Enable and Disable Alerts

You can enable or disable an alert by toggling the [switch](#alerts) on the right:

- Enabling a disabled alert causes the alert to transition to `Pending` until it is time for
  evaluation.

  Depending on the value, the alert transitions to `OK` or `Alerting`.

- Disabling an alert causes the alert to transition to `Disabled`.

## Add API Manager and Runtime Manager Alerts

In addition to creating basic and custom dashboard alerts in Anypoint Monitoring, you can navigate
from Anypoint Monitoring to the alert-creation pages in API Manager and Runtime Manager.

![Alerts menu options to set up basic, custom dashboard, or other types of alerts](_images/alerts-menu.png)

> [!NOTE] Alerts created in API Manager and Runtime Manager arenât listed in the **Alerts** page for
> Anypoint Monitoring.

1.  From **Alerts** in Anypoint Monitoring, click **\+ New alert**.
2.  Select **Other** for API Manager or Runtime Manager to access its alert configuration page.
    - **Create an alert for your API in API Manager**
    - **Create an alert for your API in Runtime Manager**

3.  To create these alerts, proceed to:
    - [Runtime Manager Alerts](../runtime-manager/alerts-on-runtime-manager)
    - [API Manager Alerts](../api-manager/latest/using-api-alerts)

## See Also

- [Basic Alerts](basic-alerts)
- [Custom Dashboard Alerts](custom-dashboard-alerts)
---

# Getting Alerts in Anypoint Monitoring

> For the full documentation index, see: https://docs.mulesoft.com/llms.txt

Use Anypoint Monitoring to create and receive alerts on your deployed applications:

- [Alerts on US and EU](alerts-us-eu) Anypoint Platform clouds
  - Set up [basic alerts](basic-alerts)
  - Set up [alerts for custom dashboards](custom-dashboard-alerts)

- [Alerts on Japan and Canada](alerts-jp-ca) Anypoint Platform clouds
---

# Setting Up Monitoring for Hybrid Deployments

> For the full documentation index, see: https://docs.mulesoft.com/llms.txt

Install the Anypoint Monitoring agent on an on-premises server to monitor applications that are
running on that server and managed in the cloud.

The Anypoint Monitoring agent sends monitoring data to the Anypoint Monitoring cloud endpoint.
Anypoint Monitoring data is transferred using an open-source protocol for transferring log data over
a highly efficient bidirectional binary channel. Data is securely transmitted using mutual TLS
encryption.

To use Anypoint Monitoring across a server group or cluster, you must install Anypoint Monitoring on
each individual server.

## Before You Begin

Before installing the Anypoint Monitoring agent, set up and run a Mule runtime engine instance on
your server. For guidance, see
[Run Mule Runtime Engine On-Premises](../mule-runtime/latest/mule-standalone).

If you have an outbound firewall, open ports based on the `am.zip` installer version for the
Anypoint Monitoring agent:

- 443 for all installer versions
- 8443 for 3.x installer versions

Outbound connections are made to the following endpoints:

- US Control Plane
  - 3.x installers:
    - us1.ingest.mulesoft.com:8443
    - data-authenticator.anypoint.mulesoft.com:443
    - s3.amazonaws.com:443

  - 2.x installers:
    - dias-ingestor-router.us-east-1.prod.cloudhub.io:443
    - data-authenticator.anypoint.mulesoft.com:443
    - s3.amazonaws.com:443

- US-GOV Control Plane
  - data-authenticator.gov.anypoint.mulesoft.com:443
  - dias-ingestor-router.us-gov-west-1.gprod.msap.io:443

- EU Control Plane
  - 3.x installers:
    - eu1.ingest.mulesoft.com:8443
    - data-authenticator.anypoint.mulesoft.com:443
    - s3.eu-central-1.amazonaws.com:443

  - 2.x installers:
    - dias-ingestor-router.eu-central-1.prod-eu.msap.io:443
    - data-authenticator.eu1.anypoint.mulesoft.com:443
    - s3.eu-central-1.amazonaws.com:443

### Ports

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Region</th><th>Name</th><th>Port</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><div><div><p><strong>US</strong></p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>us1.ingest.mulesoft.com</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>8443</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><div><div><p><strong>US</strong></p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>anypoint.mulesoft.com</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>443</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><div><div><p><strong>US</strong></p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>runtime-manager.anypoint.mulesoft.com</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>443</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><div><div><p><strong>US</strong></p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>analytics-ingest.anypoint.mulesoft.com</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>443</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><div><div><p><strong>US</strong></p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>data-authenticator.anypoint.mulesoft.com</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>443</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><div><div><p><strong>US</strong></p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>exchange-files.anypoint.mulesoft.com</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>443</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><div><div><p><strong>US</strong></p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>exchange2-asset-manager-kprod.s3.amazonaws.com</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>443</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><div><div><p><strong>US-GOV</strong></p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>runtime-manager.gov.anypoint.mulesoft.com</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>443</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><div><div><p><strong>US-GOV</strong></p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>gov.anypoint.mulesoft.com</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>443</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><div><div><p><strong>US-GOV</strong></p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>exchange-files.gov.anypoint.mulesoft.com</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>443</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><div><div><p><strong>US-GOV</strong></p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>exchange2-asset-manager-kgprod.s3.us-gov-west-1.amazonaws.com</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>443</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><div><div><p><strong>EU</strong></p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>eu1.ingest.mulesoft.com</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>8443</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><div><div><p><strong>EU</strong></p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>eu1.anypoint.mulesoft.com</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>443</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><div><div><p><strong>EU</strong></p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>runtime-manager.eu1.anypoint.mulesoft.com</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>443</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><div><div><p><strong>EU</strong></p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>analytics-ingest.eu1.anypoint.mulesoft.com</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>443</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><div><div><p><strong>EU</strong></p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>data-authenticator.eu1.anypoint.mulesoft.com</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>443</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><div><div><p><strong>EU</strong></p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>exchange2-asset-manager-kprod-eu.s3.eu-central-1.amazonaws.com</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>443</p></div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

## Supported Operating Systems

Install the Anypoint Monitoring agent on a supported operating systems:

- 3.x versions of the installer:
  - RedHat Enterprise Linux 9
  - Ubuntu 20.04
  - Windows Server 2019

- 2.x versions of the installer:
  - CentOS 7
  - RedHat Enterprise Linux 7
  - Ubuntu 16.04 and 18.04
  - Windows Server 2016, Windows Server 2019

To install the agent, you must have root or administrative privileges.

## Supported Versions of Mule Runtime Engine

Run your Mule app on a supported Mule runtime version. For supported versions, see
[Mule Runtime Release Notes](../release-notes/mule-runtime/mule-esb).

## Supported Versions of Mule Runtime Manager Agent

You must install a supported version of the Anypoint Runtime Manager agent in the Mule runtime
engine running on the server. For more information, see
[Runtime Manager Agent](../runtime-manager/runtime-manager-agent).

- 2.7.x

  An Anypoint Monitoring agent from the version 3.x installer requires Mule Runtime Manager agent
  version 2.7.1 or higher.

- 2.6.x
- 2.5.x
- 2.4.x
- 2.3.0
- 2.2.x
- 2.1.9
- 2.1.7
- 2.1.6
- 1.14.x
- 1.13.0
- 1.12.x
- 1.11.x
- 1.10.0

You must manage Mule deployments in Runtime Manager. You can add and check for your hybrid
deployments from the [Runtime Manager UI](../runtime-manager/deploying-to-your-own-servers) or the
[Runtime Manager REST API](../runtime-manager/runtime-manager-api).

## Hardware Prerequisites

Ensure that the server has the minimum hardware requirements for Anypoint Monitoring, which are:

- 2GHz CPU or 1 virtual CPU
- 1GB RAM
- 4GB disk space
- Intel x86 or x64 compatible CPU architecture

## Install the Anypoint Monitoring Agent

1.  Meet the prerequisites in [Before You Begin](#prereqs):

    Mule runtime engine must be installed and running.

2.  In the sidebar, click **Settings**.
3.  To monitor applications deployed to a local server, select **Hybrid**, and follow the procedure
    to install Anypoint Monitoring agent from the latest version of the agentâs installer in
    `am.zip`.
    - When you extract the `am.zip` file, a folder called `am` is created in the location where you
      extracted the ZIP.
    - The installation script asks if you want Anypoint Monitoring installed as a service.
      - If you donât want to have it preset as a service by the installer, select **No**.
      - You can call the installation script with the `-x` argument to skip this prompt. If you skip
        the prompt, Anypoint Monitoring is not installed as a service.

    - You cannot install the Anypoint Monitoring agent on a server that has an existing filebeat
      instance running.
    - When you install the Anypoint Monitoring agent on a hybrid server in a server group, use
      Runtime Manager to restart the server to enable Anypoint Monitoring to begin collecting
      metrics.
    - To change the telemetry listening port (defaults to port `8888`), set the `-t` parameter to an
      integer from 1024 through 65535.

      Example: `$ ./install -t 8889`.

    - To install multiple instances of the agent on a Linux host, see
      [Install Multiple Agents to a Linux Host](#install-multiple-agents).
    - For traces in Anypoint Monitoring, see
      [Enable Trace Data Collection for Hybrid Runtime Servers](#tracing-setup).

### Install Multiple Agents to a Linux Host

On a Linux host, you can install multiple agent instances:

- Non-service installations: To install multiple agent instances, assign a unique port to each agent
  instance.

  Set the `-t` parameter to an integer from 1024 through 65535.

  Example: `$ ./install -t 8889`

- Service installations: To install multiple agent instances as services, assign a unique port and
  installation name to each instance.
  - For the first service installation, you can use the default configuration. The `-t` and `-n`
    parameters are optional.
  - For subsequent service installations, you must use both `-t` and `-n`.

    Example: `$ ./install -t <port> -n <installation-name>`

    The installer prompts you for a unique port and installation name.

Parameters:

- `-t`: Sets the port number to an integer from 1024 through 65535. The default is 8888.
- `-n`: Sets a unique name that becomes part of the service name. The name must be no more than
  eight alphanumeric characters. The default is an unnamed port. Required for the second and
  subsequent agent instances on a single Linux host. Supported with `-t` only for service
  installations.

## Enable Trace Data Collection for Hybrid Runtime Servers

<table><colgroup><col></colgroup><tbody><tr><td><p><span><img src="_images/info.png" alt="info" width="15" height="15" title="This feature requires an Advanced or Titanium subscription."></span> Availability: <a href="./#billing">Advanced and Titanium tiers only</a></p></td></tr></tbody></table>

For installer version 3.2.14 or later, you can enable trace data collection for Anypoint Monitoring.
On each hybrid server where you want to collect trace data, configure a JVM property and install
agent with the trace data collection option. Version 3.2.1.4 was released on
[March 5, 2025](../release-notes/monitoring/anypoint-monitoring-installer-release-notes#march-5-2026).

1.  Upgrade to an Anypoint Monitoring agent version that supports trace data collection
    (am-3.2.14.0.zip or later).
2.  Stop Mule.
3.  Add this line to `$MULE_HOME/conf/wrapper.conf`:

    `wrapper.java.additional.<N>=-Dmule.openTelemetry.tracer.exporter.enabled=true`

    `<N>`: The next available property index.

4.  Install the agent using the `-g 4317` option:

    `$MULE_HOME/am/bin/install -g 4317`

    Mule runtime uses port 4317 for trace data collection by default (see
    [OpenTelemetry Support in Mule Runtime](../mule-runtime/latest/otel-support)).

5.  Restart Mule.
6.  Finish setting up the agent:

    `$MULE_HOME/am/bin/setup`

## Proxy Settings

If you proxy your monitoring data as it leaves the data center, you must configure the Anypoint
Monitoring agent to communicate through the proxy.

The configuration and supported protocol depends on the version of the Anypoint Monitoring agentâs
installer:

- [3.x](#v3): Supports the HTTP/HTTPS protocol and does not support SOCKS5
- [2.x](#v2): Supports the SOCKS5 protocol and does not support HTTP/HTTPS

See also, [Supported Operating Systems](#os-support).

### Configure Proxy Communication for Monitoring Agents from Version 3.x Installers (HTTP/HTTPS Protocol)

If you proxy your monitoring data as it leaves the data center and youâre using an Anypoint
Monitoring agent from a _version 3.x_ installer, the proxy must support the HTTP/HTTPS protocol, and
you must configure the Anypoint Monitoring agent to communicate through the proxy.

To configure the agent for an HTTP or HTTPS proxy:

1.  Set up Runtime Manager with a proxy. See
    [Create a Server in Runtime Manager](../runtime-manager/servers-create).
2.  Install the Anypoint Monitoring agent and use the `-p` option. For example, on a Linux server,
    at the command line, enter:

    $ ./install -p https://user:password@https-server:1080

    For a user or password of the proxy configuration that contains URI-reserved characters, you
    must percent-encode the characters according to the encoding rules of RFC 3986.

### Configure Proxy Communication for Monitoring Agents from Version 2.x Installers (SOCKS5 Protocol)

If you proxy your monitoring data as it leaves the data center and youâre using an Anypoint
Monitoring agent from a _version 3.x_ installer, the proxy must support the SOCKS5 protocol, and you
must configure the Anypoint Monitoring agent to communicate through the proxy.

To configure the agent for the SOCKS5 proxy:

1.  Set up Runtime Manager with a proxy. See
    [Create a Server in Runtime Manager](../runtime-manager/servers-create).
2.  Install the Anypoint Monitoring agent and use the `-p` option. For example, on a Windows server,
    at the command line, enter:

    <location-of-am-folder\\bin>powershell -file install.ps1 -p
    socks5://user:password@socks5-server:1080

    For a Linux server, enter:

    $ ./install -p socks5://user:password@socks5-server:1080

    For a user or password of the proxy configuration that contains URI-reserved characters, you
    must percent-encode the characters according to the encoding rules of RFC 3986.

## Verify the Anypoint Monitoring Agent is Running

1.  Wait 10 minutes for the configuration to propagate.
2.  Log in to Anypoint Platform with a user account that has
    [permission to access Anypoint Monitoring content](am-permissions).
3.  Navigate to Anypoint Monitoring:

    Click **Monitoring** from the Anypoint Platform home page or from the **Runtimes** menu in the
    header.

4.  On the **Built-in Dashboards** page, select an app that is deployed on the server where you
    installed the Anypoint Monitoring agent.
5.  Check whether the CPU Utilization and Memory Utilization charts are displaying data.  
    If you see data in the charts, the Anypoint Monitoring agent is installed and running.  
    If you do not see data in the charts, verify that Anypoint Monitoring is able to connect to the
    endpoint.

    > [!NOTE] If necessary, you can add the endpoint to an allowlist for outbound firewall rules so
    > your server allows connections. The endpoints to add to the allowlist are listed in
    > [Before You Begin](#prereqs).

6.  Log into the server on which the Anypoint Monitoring agent is running.
7.  Open the log file for the data-transfer agent:
    - 3.x log file path: `./am/logs`

      If this agent is running as a service on Linux, the log file path is
      `/var/log/otel-collector`.

    - 2.x log file path: `/var/log/filebeat`

      If this agent is running as a service on Linux, the log file path is /var/log/filebeat\`.

8.  Look for entries that mention connection failures or connection retries.

## Update the Anypoint Monitoring Agent

When you upgrade the Runtime Manager agent, you must reinstall the Anypoint Monitoring agent.

If you need to update the Anypoint Monitoring agent at some point in the future, follow these steps:

1.  Stop Mule.
2.  In the `am` folder, run the following command:  
    `./bin/uninstall`
3.  Delete the `am` folder.
4.  Follow the instructions for
    [installing the Anypoint Monitoring agent](#install_ap_monitoring_onprem) on-premises.
5.  Restart Mule.

> [!NOTE] If you add or remove a Mule runtime from the cluster or a server group, you must reinstall
> the Anypoint Monitoring agent.

## Performance Impact

Enabling monitoring can impact CPU utilization and memory utilization depending on your specific
application configuration. Before enabling monitoring in your production environment, test any
performance impact in your pre-production environment to ensure optimal performance and sizing.

## Troubleshooting

- If you find connection failure or retry entries, ensure that port 8443 (Anypoint Monitoring agents
  from installer version 3.x and later only) and 443 (all Anypoint Monitoring agents) in your
  outbound firewall are open.
- If you donât find connection failure or retry entries, contact the support team.
- On Windows, if Anypoint Monitoring agent is configured to run as a service and throws Error 1064
  when starting, find the root cause by opening the Windows application Events Viewer to **Windows
  Logs** â **Applications**.
- If you encounter inconsistent path normalization, refer to [Normalize API Paths](#normalize-path).

## Disable Log Forwarding for Hybrid Runtime Servers

<table><colgroup><col></colgroup><tbody><tr><td><p><span><img src="_images/info.png" alt="info" width="15" height="15" title="This feature requires an Advanced or Titanium subscription."></span> Availability: <a href="./#billing">Advanced and Titanium tiers only</a></p></td></tr></tbody></table>

You can disable log forwarding to Anypoint Monitoring if you have AM installer v.2.5.10 or later. To
disable this feature, you must configure a JVM property on each hybrid server for which you want to
disable log forwarding. You cannot disable log forwarding per application.

To disable log forwarding:

1.  Upgrade the Anypoint Monitoring agent to a version that supports disabling log forwarding
    (am-2.5.10.0.zip or later).
2.  Stop Mule.
3.  Add the following line in the `MULE_HOME/conf/wrapper.conf` file (where `<N>` is the next
    available property index):  
    `wrapper.java.additional.<N>=-Danypoint.platform.monitoring.logs_forwarding.enabled=false`
4.  Restart Mule.

## Normalize API Paths

Proper path normalization helps to ensure correct counting of requests to inbound HTTP endpoints in
Runtime Manager and Anypoint Monitoring dashboards. Normalization enables charts in these dashboards
to ignore insignificant differences, typically from dynamic variables, between URLs that make
requests to the same endpoint.

For inbound HTTP endpoints, you can rely on the [default path normalization](#normalization-default)
or configure a [custom normalization pattern](#normalization-custom) with the
`anypoint.platform.config.analytics.endpoint.patterns` property.

Supported deployment types:

- CloudHub
- CloudHub 2.0
- Hybrid
- Runtime Fabric

### Default Path Normalization

The default path normalization process follows these substitution patterns for:

- Digits: Replace any part of the path containing a digit with `X`. For example,
  `/booking/abc123/pax/A1B2C4` becomes `/booking/X/pax/X`.
- Uppercase segments: Replace any part of the path containing uppercase characters but no lower case
  characters with `X`. For example, `/booking/ABC/pax/A1B2C4` becomes `/booking/X/pax/X`.

Parts that contain a mix of upper case and lower case characters remain the same. For example,
`/booking/abc123/pax/PAisdf` remains `/booking/X/pax/PAisdf`.

### Configure a Custom Path Normalization Pattern

To override the default behavior, you can configure
`anypoint.platform.config.analytics.endpoint.patterns` on a running Mule app in Runtime Manager or
when deploying a new application:

1.  Log in to your Anypoint Platform account and select **Runtime Manager**.
2.  Either click **Deploy Application** for a new deployment, or select an existing application and
    click **Manage Application**.
3.  Navigate to the **Properties** tab in the **Settings** section.
4.  Under **New Key**, add `anypoint.platform.config.analytics.endpoint.patterns`.
5.  Under **New Value**, add the pattern for your dynamic endpoint, for example,
    `/api/V1/proc/payments/{paymentId}/cancel`.
6.  Click **Apply Changes**.

By defining an explicit pattern, you can ensure consistent normalization of dynamic endpoints. All
incoming requests matching the pattern are correctly normalized to a single endpoint.

For example, provide a pattern that identifies the variable part.

For variables, you can use:

- `{}` with an empty string
- `{string}` with a string value

For example, assume property values that follow these patterns:

- `/v1/payments/{paymentId}/cancel` (single property)
- `/v1/order/{orderId},/v1/order/{}/cancel` (multiple properties separated by `,`)

These incoming requests match one of the defined patterns:

- `/v1/order/jAXjh`
- `/v1/payments/CAjjAXjh/cancel`
- `/v1/order/jAXjh/cancel`

Normalized results for the example requests:

- `/v1/payments/X/cancel`
- `/v1/order/X`
- `/v1/order/X/cancel`

## Restart After Changing Subscription Tiers

For the _2.x version_ of Anypoint Monitoring agent, changes to your subscription tier require
further action to continue collecting metrics.

To trigger metrics collection through a 2.x agent after changing your subscription:

1.  Stop Mule runtime engine.
2.  Uninstall and reinstall the agent.

This action isnât required for the 3.x version of the agent. Version 3.x maintains persistent
monitoring connectivity and adapts to backend infrastructure updates dynamically.

## See Also

- [Anypoint Monitoring Settings](monitoring-settings-page)
- [Download and Install Mule 4](../mule-runtime/latest/runtime-installation-task)
- [Installing and Configuring Mule Runtime Manager Agent](../runtime-manager/installing-and-configuring-runtime-manager-agent)

# Managing Permissions for Anypoint Monitoring

> For the full documentation index, see: https://docs.mulesoft.com/llms.txt

Anypoint Monitoring has product-specific permissions that control access to its assets:

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Required Permissions</th><th>Grants the Ability to</th><th>Notes</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><div><div><p>Monitoring Administrator</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>View, create, modify, and delete content in Anypoint Monitoring.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>&nbsp;</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><div><div><p>Monitoring Viewer</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>View but not modify content in Anypoint Monitoring.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>&nbsp;</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><div><div><p>Telemetry Exporter Administrator</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p>View connections and configurations in Telemetry Exporter.</p></li><li><p>Create, modify, and delete connections in Telemetry Exporter.</p></li><li><p>Create, modify, and delete configurations in Telemetry Exporter.</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Assign this permission at the root organization level.</p></div><div><p>Availability of this permission depends on your organizationâs subscription tier.</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><div><div><p>Telemetry Exporter Configurations Manager</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p>View connections and configurations in Telemetry Exporter.</p></li><li><p>Create, modify, and delete configurations in Telemetry Exporter.</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Availability of this permission depends on your organizationâs subscription tier.</p></div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

In addition to Monitoring Administrator and Anypoint Monitoring Viewer permissions, asset-specific
permissions are required to view and read API and app data for some deployments. For example, if you
have the Anypoint Monitoring Viewer permission and want to view Anypoint Monitoring data on a hybrid
Mule app deployment, you must also have the Read Applications permission (from Runtime Manager) in
the same environment that the app is deployed to.

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Deployment Type</th><th>Permission</th><th>Grants the Ability to</th><th>Permission Type</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><div><div><p>Hybrid Deployment Data</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Read Applications</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>View app data for hybrid deployments. See also, <a href="am-installing">Setting Up Monitoring for Hybrid Deployments</a>.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p><a href="../access-management/permissions-by-product#runtime-manager">Runtime Manager</a></p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td rowspan="2"><div><div><p>Omni Gateway (formerly Flex Gateway) Deployment Data</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>View API Alerts</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>View the API alerts for Flex API data.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p><a href="../access-management/permissions-by-product#api-manager">API Manager</a></p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><div><div><p>Read Servers</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>View server and Omni Gateway (formerly Flex Gateway) resources.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p><a href="../access-management/permissions-by-product#runtime-manager">Runtime Manager</a></p></div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>
