2.  From the dashboardâs header, click ![](_images/more-menu-vert-ellipsis.png) (**More menu**), and
    select **Duplicate**.
3.  Save a **New Name** for the dashboard.

    Your copy of the dashboard opens.

## Delete a Custom Dashboard

Delete a custom dashboard you no longer need.

1.  From **Custom Dashboards**, open a custom dashboard.

    For guidance, see [View a Custom Dashboard](#view).

2.  From the dashboardâs header, click ![](_images/more-menu-vert-ellipsis.png) (**More menu**), and
    select **Delete**.
3.  Confirm the deletion.

## Manage Custom Dashboard Charts

Manage a chart from its ![](_images/more-menu-vert-ellipsis.png) (**More menu**).

![Chart More menu](_images/dashboard-custom-chart-more-hf.png)

- Collapse or expand the chart.
- Configure the chart:
  - [Configuring Graphs in a Custom Dashboard](dashboard-custom-config-graph)
  - [Configuring Singlestats in a Custom Dashboard](dashboard-custom-config-singlestat)
  - [Configuring Tables in a Custom Dashboard](dashboard-custom-config-table)
  - [Configuring Text Panels in a Custom Dashboard](dashboard-custom-config-text)

- Duplicate the chart.
- Add or edit a graphâs alert. Other chart types donât support alerts.
- Export graph or table data to a CSV file. Other chart types donât support CSV exports.

  For more information, see [Exporting App Data to a CSV File](export-app-data-to-csv).

- Delete the chart.
---

# Monitoring Metrics from Dashboards

> For the full documentation index, see: https://docs.mulesoft.com/llms.txt

Dashboards in Anypoint Monitoring provide visibility into Mule apps and APIs that are deployed to
your environments (for example, Production, Sandbox, or Design).

## Built-In Dashboards

Built-in dashboards contain graphs that plot current and historical data collected over a given time
and date period. There is a built-in dashboard for each Mule application and API in each
environment.

![Built-in dashboard example](_images/intro-dashboard-built-in.png)

Figure 1. Example: Built-in Dashboard for a Mule Application

## Custom Dashboards

<table><colgroup><col></colgroup><tbody><tr><td><p><span><img src="_images/info.png" alt="info" width="15" height="15" title="This feature requires an Advanced or Titanium subscription."></span> Availability: <a href="./#billing">Advanced and Titanium tiers only</a></p></td></tr></tbody></table>

Custom dashboards in Anypoint Monitoring can bring together important metrics and data points that
you need to see on one screen. You can specify the resources and metrics that you want to monitor,
allowing you to:

- Correlate diverse metrics
- Perform comparative analysis
- Differentiate between regular trends and anomalies
- Isolate issues quickly

For example, you can compare live data with historic data to detect anomalies and expedite the
troubleshooting process.

## Causes of No Data Points Messages for Dashboards

A dashboard in Anypoint Monitoring might return **No data points** instead of metrics. The following
scenarios commonly cause the **No data points** message to appear:

- You selected an incorrect time range.

  For example, the app started yesterday, and you selected a day from the past week as your time
  range.

- You removed the Monitoring property from the runtime.
- The application for which you are trying to view metrics has stopped.
- You selected a time range that is outside of the retention policy of a metric.

  For example, API path or client IP metrics show only the last hour, and you selected yesterday as
  the time range.

- You used an incorrect query.

  For example, when using custom dashboards, you selected two app IDs at the same time using the
  query `WHERE app_id = app_1 AND app_id = app_2`.

## See Also

- [Built-in Application Dashboards](app-dashboards)
- [Configuring Custom Dashboards](dashboard-custom-config)
- [API Analytics Dashboard](api-dashboard)
---

# Exporting App Data to a CSV File

> For the full documentation index, see: https://docs.mulesoft.com/llms.txt

In built-in and custom dashboards, you can export chart data to a CSV file. If you apply a filter to
a dashboard, the exported data includes only the data included in the filter you set, such as a
time-range filter.

1.  Log in to Anypoint Platform with a user account that has
    [permission to access Anypoint Monitoring content](am-permissions).
2.  Navigate to Anypoint Monitoring:

    Click **Monitoring** from the Anypoint Platform home page or from the **Runtimes** menu in the
    header.

3.  In the sidebar, click **Built-in dashboards** or **Custom dashboards**.
4.  Select the application for which to download the data.
5.  Specify the time range for the data to download.
6.  In the chart that contains the data to download, click the
    ![](_images/more-menu-vert-ellipsis.png) (**More menu**), and select **Export CSV**.
7.  In the **Export CSV** dialog, specify the way to format the data.
    - In **Mode**, select the format for the data:
      - **Series as rows** formats the data in rows in the CSV file.
      - **Series as columns** formats the data as columns in the CSV file.

    - In **Date Time Format**, enter the values to specify the date format, for example:
      `YYYY-MM-DDTHH:mm:ssZ`
    - Determine whether to use **Excel CSV dialect** (enabled by default).

8.  Click **Export**.
---

# Anypoint Monitoring Overview

> For the full documentation index, see: https://docs.mulesoft.com/llms.txt

Understanding the state of your infrastructure and systems is important for ensuring the reliability
and stability of your services. As part of Anypoint Platform, Anypoint Monitoring provides
visibility into integrations across your app network. The monitoring tools provide feedback from
Mule flows and components in your app network.

Operations and development teams use the monitoring tools to diagnose issues and prescribe solutions
to behavior that negatively impacts digital performance. The monitoring tools are designed to reduce
the time to identify and resolve these issues through aggregated metrics, data visualization tools,
alerts for issues, and a log aggregation system. Specifically, Anypoint Monitoring provides ways to:

- Aggregate and map metrics across dependent systems in real-time.
- Configure dashboards and alerts to reduce the mean time to identification of issues (MTTI).
- Store and search log data at scale.

## Monitoring Features by Control Plane

Anypoint Monitoring feature availability depends on the control plane for your Anypoint Platform
instance and your [Anypoint Platform package or plan](#billing). Data isnât shared across the
separate instances of Anypoint Platform.

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Anypoint Monitoring Feature</th><th><a href="https://anypoint.mulesoft.com/monitoring">US</a>, <a href="https://eu1.anypoint.mulesoft.com/monitoring">EU</a> Clouds</th><th><a href="https://ca1.platform.mulesoft.com/monitoring">Canada</a>, <a href="https://jp1.platform.mulesoft.com/monitoring">Japan</a> Clouds (on Hyperforce)</th><th>Government Cloud</th><th>Private Cloud (3.x)</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><div><div><p></p></div><div><ul><li><p><a href="dashboards">Built-in and Custom dashboards</a></p></li><li><p><a href="anypoint-custom-metrics-connector">Custom metrics</a></p></li><li><p><a href="tools">Tools</a> (Notifications)</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Yes</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>No</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Yes</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Yes</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><div><div><p></p></div><div><p><a href="#alerts">Alerts</a></p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Yes</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Yes</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Yes</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Yes</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><div><div><p><a href="#insights">Anypoint Insights</a></p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Yes</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Yes</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>No</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>No</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><div><div><p><a href="#functional-monitoring">API Functional Monitoring</a></p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Yes</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Yes</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>No</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>No</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><div><div><p><a href="#logging">Logging features</a></p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Yes</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Yes</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Yes</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>No</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><div><div><p><a href="reports">Reports</a></p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Yes</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Yes</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Yes</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Yes</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><div><div><p><a href="telemetry-exporter">Telemetry exports</a></p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Yes</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Yes</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>No</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>No</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><div><div><p><a href="traces">Traces</a></p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Yes</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>No</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>No</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>No</p></div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

For more information about MuleSoft Government Cloud and Anypoint Platform Private Cloud Edition
(Anypoint Platform PCE), see
[Control Plane Hosting Options](../hosting-home/#control-plane-hosting-options).

### Anypoint Insights

Monitor performance data and issues across your deployed Mule apps and APIs (_entities_), and get
detailed insights on specific deployments.

See [Monitoring Metrics from Anypoint Insights](anypoint-insights).

### Event-Driven Alerts

<table><colgroup><col></colgroup><tbody><tr><td><p><span><img src="_images/info.png" alt="info" width="15" height="15" title="This feature requires an Advanced or Core-based subscription."></span> Availability: <a href="#billing">Advanced and Core-based tiers only</a></p></td></tr></tbody></table>

Configure alerts to receive notifications about abnormal behavior and problems in your app network.

You can trigger alert notifications based on thresholds you define for your Mule apps and APIs.

See [Getting Alerts in Anypoint Monitoring](alerts).

### Log Management

Manage logs and log data for your deployments with these logging features:

- [Log Searches](#log-search)
- [Log Points](#log-points)
- [Raw Data Downloads](#raw-data-downloads)

#### Log Searches

<table><colgroup><col></colgroup><tbody><tr><td><p><span><img src="_images/info.png" alt="info" width="15" height="15" title="This feature requires a Usage-based or Titanium subscription."></span> Availability: <a href="#billing">Usage-based and Titanium tiers only</a></p></td></tr></tbody></table>

Search for and review logs from across your deployed applications.

See [Using Logs in Anypoint Monitoring](logs).

> [!NOTE] Log search limited to individual apps is available to all subscription tiers in Runtime
> Manager for apps deployed to CloudHub and CloudHub 2.0. You can navigate to Runtime Manager, open
> the app, and then click **Logs**.
>
> The log aggregator system holds raw operational data and records events that occur in the
> operating system or Mule app. Log aggregation compiles, organizes, and indexes log files to
> facilitate management, searching, and analysis. Aggregated logs complement the monitoring system
> by helping you identify causes of operational issues and investigate failures.
>
> For more logging options, see
> [Logging by Deployment Model](../runtime-manager/deployment-strategies#logging).

#### Log Points

<table><colgroup><col></colgroup><tbody><tr><td><p><span><img src="_images/info.png" alt="info" width="15" height="15" title="This feature requires an Advanced or Titanium subscription."></span> Availability: <a href="#billing">Advanced and Titanium tiers only</a></p></td></tr></tbody></table>

In the US and EU Clouds, generate logs for Mule apps and APIs in real-time and without writing code.

See [Generating Real-Time Logs (US and EU)](log-points).

#### Raw Data Downloads

<table><colgroup><col></colgroup><tbody><tr><td><p><span><img src="_images/info.png" alt="info" width="15" height="15" title="This feature requires an Advanced or Titanium subscription."></span> Availability: <a href="#billing">Advanced and Titanium tiers only</a></p></td></tr></tbody></table>

In the US and EU Clouds, download warehoused (archived) log data for a Mule app, for example, to
help investigate security, compliance, or other issues that logs can reveal.

See [Downloading Raw Data (US and EU)](raw-data).

### Tools for Monitoring Integrations

<table><colgroup><col></colgroup><tbody><tr><td><p><span><img src="_images/info.png" alt="info" width="15" height="15" title="This feature requires an Advanced or Titanium subscription."></span> Availability: <a href="#billing">Advanced and Titanium tiers only</a></p></td></tr></tbody></table>

Use connectors that help deliver Anypoint Monitoring notifications to external systems, including
custom metrics connectors.

See [Sending Notifications to External Systems](tools).

### API Functional Monitoring

Monitor APIs and endpoints at scheduled intervals. Receive reports about whether tests pass or fail,
and view statistics about API and endpoint performance.

See [API Functional Monitoring](../api-functional-monitoring/afm-in-anypoint-platform).

## Data Storage and Retention

Data storage and retention limits are determined by your pricing plan. If your organization has the
Anypoint Integration Advanced package or a Titanium subscription to Anypoint Platform, this feature
provides a higher resource allocation.

- For the Anypoint Integration Starter package or the Gold and Platinum subscriptions:
  - Stores metric data in the same region as your control plane

- For the Anypoint Integration Advanced package or Titanium subscription:
  - Significantly increases the storage
  - Stores log and metric data in the same region as your control plane

See [Anypoint Platform Packages and Plans](#billing). For complete details about pricing, contact
your customer support representative.

## Anypoint Platform Packages and Plans

Feature availability in Anypoint Monitoring depends on the tier of your Anypoint Platform pricing
package or plan:

- [Anypoint Monitoring Features for Usage-Based Packages](#plans-usage-based) (Starter and Advanced
  tiers)
- [Anypoint Monitoring Features for Legacy Core-Based Plans](#plans-legacy) (Gold, Platinum, and
  Titanium tiers)

### Anypoint Monitoring Features for Usage-Based Packages

Anypoint Monitoring provides standard tooling for monitoring Mule app and API performance so that
you can more quickly identify and resolve issues.

- **Anypoint Integration Starter** package is available to all Anypoint Platform regions.
- **Anypoint Integration Advanced** package is available in the
  [US](https://anypoint.mulesoft.com/monitoring) and
  [EU](https://eu1.anypoint.mulesoft.com/monitoring) regions only at this time.

All organizations receive fundamental monitoring regardless of pricing model. However, organizations
that have the Anypoint Integration Advanced package receive greater storage, guaranteed resources,
and advanced capabilities that allow for greater control and flexibility. For more information, see
[Anypoint Platform pricing](https://www.mulesoft.com/anypoint-pricing).

If your organization changes its subscription model from the Anypoint Advanced Integration package
to the Anypoint Integration Starter package, ensure you have preserved your logs outside of Anypoint
Platform. Within an hour of changing pricing models, MuleSoft clears the storage that formerly kept
log data.

These features are available for Anypoint Platform customers who have usage-based plans:

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Feature</th><th>Anypoint Integration Starter Package</th><th>Anypoint Integration Advanced > [!NOTE] Anypoint Visualizer app network integration refers to features in the **Application
> Network** tab within Anypoint Monitoring. Outside of Anypoint Monitoring, the product Anypoint
> Visualizer is available to organizations in all core-based subscription tiers (Gold, Platinum,
> Titanium).

### Anypoint Monitoring Features for Legacy Core-Based Plans

Core-based subscriptions are based on vCore allotments instead of usage. All organizations receive
fundamental monitoring capabilities with their subscriptions. However, customers subscribing at the
Titanium tier receive greater storage, guaranteed resources, and advanced capabilities that allow
for greater control and flexibility.

If your organization changes its subscription model from a Titanium to a Gold or Platinum
subscription, ensure you have preserved your logs outside of Anypoint Platform. Within an hour of
changing subscription models, MuleSoft clears the storage that formerly kept log data.

NOTE:

API Visualizer (formerly Anypoint Visualizer) app network integration refers to features in the
**Application Network** tab within Anypoint Monitoring. API Visualizer as a product outside of
Anypoint Monitoring is available to organizations regardless of pricing plan.

## Use Cases

The following use cases provide examples of how you can use Anypoint Monitoring.

- Understand performance-related KPIs  
  DevOps engineer Gary maintains a series of APIs related to a transaction processing system.
  Because the TPS requires swift response, Gary uses API response-time dashboards and alerts to send
  an email when a bottleneck occurs. After gathering some data, Gary can then size his apps.
- Plan resource allocation across the app network

  IT specialist Peyton uses API Visualizer to view their app network. For CloudHub and Anypoint
  Runtime Fabric apps, Peyton sees CPU and memory utilization and idle capacity using integrated
  Anypoint Monitoring.

- Pinpoint root causes of issues  
  Support team member Darius receives a call that the system is running slowly. He examines
  dashboards and checks response times, failed requests, and so on. He identifies that the backend
  is not scaling for the number of requests; he can add capacity for Mule apps, or he can throttle
  requests for other backend issues.
- Measure API trends  
  Architect Kara monitors several APIs using dashboards, and she reports API call trends to
  leadership. She creates a dashboard that shows her top 10 APIâs trends per month so she can easily
  identify APIs that have high volumes of calls or issues.
- Assess the stability of APIs  
  Operations administrator Vic can periodically assess the error rate and success rate of key APIs
  using aggregated views and computations in Anypoint Monitoring. Based on their observations, Vic
  can stabilize or redesign apps that perform poorly.
- Analyze logs  
  Support engineer Carole searches through Anypoint Monitoring logs and notices some apps frequently
  have errors. She uses this data to create dashboards for areas that have frequent issues, and she
  creates a plan to improve end processes based on the types of errors identified in the logs.
- Visualize regional or project-based consumption  
  Business owner Devon has apps across different runtime planes. He has aggregated dashboards of the
  regional or project-specific apps, and he can easily determine what is consuming the most
  resources and evangelize accordingly.

## See Also

- [Create a Monitor](../api-functional-monitoring/afm-create-monitor)
---

# Getting Analytics and Insights with Integration Intelligence

> For the full documentation index, see: https://docs.mulesoft.com/llms.txt

[![](../_/img/icons/info.svg)](/monitoring/#billing)

Use dashboards in the Salesforce ecosystem to analyze Mule app and API metrics, and get insights
into related business data.

![Integration Intelligence Dashboards](_images/ii-dashboards.png)

<table><tbody><tr><td><i data-value="1"></i><b>1</b></td><td>Integration Intelligence in the Salesforce Integration app</td></tr><tr><td><i data-value="2"></i><b>2</b></td><td><strong>Dashboard</strong> page in Integration Intelligence</td></tr><tr><td><i data-value="3"></i><b>3</b></td><td>Built-in dashboards: <strong>Apps</strong> (default), <strong>APIs</strong>, <strong>Business Insights</strong></td></tr><tr><td><i data-value="4"></i><b>4</b></td><td>Filters for selecting a <a href="#lookback">time period</a>, apps, and deployment types, such as CloudHub 2.0, in the <strong>Apps</strong> dashboard</td></tr><tr><td><i data-value="5"></i><b>5</b></td><td><a href="#dashboards-custom">Navigation to Tableau Next</a> for custom dashboard setup and management<div><p>This buttonâs label changes from <strong>Create Dashboard</strong> to <strong>Manage Dashboard</strong> after you provide a dedicated workspace in Tableau Next for custom dashboards in Integration Intelligence.</p></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

## Before You Begin

Complete the procedures in [Setting Up Integration Intelligence](integration-intelligence-setup).

## Supported Deployment Types

For metrics in dashboards, Integration Intelligence supports Mule apps and Mule APIs deployed to
these hosts:

- CloudHub (**CLOUDHUB**)
- CloudHub 2.0 (**CLOUDHUB2**)
- Runtime Fabric (**RTF**)
- Omni Gateway (formerly Flex Gateway, **FLEX**)

For logs in Integration Intelligence, see
[Supported Deployment Types for Logs](integration-intelligence-logs#supported-deployment-types).

For information about hosts, see
[Runtime Plane Hosting Options](../hosting-home/#runtime-plane-hosting-options).

## Lookback Period

Data collection for dashboards begins after you set up Integration Intelligence.

From the **Time Period** filter, you can select from predefined ranges for the year (including the
fiscal year), month, week, day, or quarter (including the fiscal quarter), or you can select a
custom date range. The custom date range selector also indicates the available date range for your
data.

## Find Dashboards in Integration Intelligence

Integration Intelligence is accessible from Anypoint Platform and through the Salesforce App
Launcher:

- [Navigate to Dashboards from Anypoint Platform](#nav-insights)
- [Navigate to Dashboards from the Salesforce App Launcher](#nav-salesforce)

> [!IMPORTANT] After initial ingestion, data can take up to 10 minutes to appear in Integration
> Intelligence. The first time you open **Dashboards**, it can take a few additional minutes for the
> visualizations to load.

### Navigate to Dashboards from Anypoint Platform

Navigate from Anypoint Platform to Integration Intelligence to review dashboards with metrics on
your Mule apps, APIs, and business data.

![Banner for navigating to Integration Intelligence from Anypoint Insights](_images/ii-go-to-ii.png)

1.  Meet the prerequisites in [Before You Begin](#prereqs).
2.  Log in to Anypoint Platform with a user account that has
    [permission to access Anypoint Monitoring content](am-permissions).
3.  Navigate to Anypoint Monitoring:

    Click **Monitoring** from the Anypoint Platform home page or from the **Runtimes** menu in the
    header.

4.  From the banner in the Anypoint Insights header, click **Go to Integration Intelligence**.

    Integration Intelligence automatically opens to **Dashboards**, and **Logs** is accessible from
    the Integration Intelligence sidebar.

5.  Select a tab for the dashboard you want to examine:
    - [**Apps**](#apps) (default)
    - [**APIs**](#apis)
    - [**Business Insights**](#business-insights)
    - A [custom dashboard](#dashboards-custom) created by your team in Tableau Next

### Navigate to Dashboards from the Salesforce App Launcher

Integration Intelligence resides in the Integration app. If youâre connected to the Salesforce org
that supports your MuleSoft telemetry data, you can navigate directly to your Integration
Intelligence dashboards from the App Launcher:

1.  Meet the prerequisites in [Before You Begin](#prereqs).
2.  From your Salesforce environment, click ![](_images/ii-app-launcher-icon.png) (App Launcher).
3.  From the App Launcher, find and select the **Integration** app.

    The launcher opens to the appâs **Intelligence** tab, which contains your dashboards.

4.  Select a tab for the dashboard you want to examine:
    - [**Apps**](#apps) (default)
    - [**APIs**](#apis)
    - [**Business Insights**](#business-insights)
    - A [custom dashboard](#dashboards-custom) created by your team in Tableau Next

## Analyze Your Mule Apps in a Built-in Dashboard

Analyze Mule app metrics in Integration Intelligence:

1.  Navigate to the **Apps** dashboard.

    For guidance, see [Find Dashboards in Integration Intelligence](#nav-ii).

2.  Optionally, narrow the scope of apps to review by:
    - Selecting a range of time to review from the **Time Period** menu.
    - Selecting one or more Mule apps from the **App** menu.
    - Selecting a deployment type, such as **CloudHub 2.0** or **Runtime Fabric**, from the
      **Deployment** menu.

3.  Get app metrics:
    - **Performance Metrics**: Get the total number of apps (**Total Apps**), number of apps with
      errors (**Apps with Error**), total number of requests to the apps (**Total App Requests**),
      and the error rate for the apps (**App Error Rate**).
    - **Apps by Deployment Type**: Hover over the data in the chart to get the number of apps by
      type, such as apps deployed to CloudHub 2.0.
    - **Top CPU Utilization**: Check performance by identifying apps that are using the most CPU
      resources.
    - **Deployed**: Check average response time, error rate, and request volume of apps over the
      selected lookback period.

## Analyze Your APIs in a Built-in Dashboard

Analyze API metrics in Integration Intelligence:

1.  Navigate to the **API** dashboard.

    For guidance, see [Find Dashboards in Integration Intelligence](#nav-ii).

2.  Get metrics on your APIs:
    - **Performance Metrics**: Get the total number of APIs (**Total APIs**), number of APIs with
      errors (**APIs with Error**), total number of requests to the APIs (**Total API Requests**),
      and the error rate for the APIs (**API Error Rate**).
    - **Request Volume by Location**: View the relative request volume by region, such as United
      States.
    - **Top API Consumers by Endpoint**: View relative API endpoint requests ordered by APIs with
      the most to least requests.
    - **APIs**: Get the number of clients and request volume for one or more versions of your APIs.

3.  To get more detail, such as the number of requests, hover over the data in the charts.

## Get Business Insights from a Built-in Dashboard

Get business metrics in Integration Intelligence:

1.  Navigate to the **Business Insights** dashboard.

    For guidance, see [Find Dashboards in Integration Intelligence](#nav-ii).

2.  Optionally, select one or more apps to review from the **App** menu.
3.  Get business insights for your organization:
    - **Top 10 Apps by Flow Count**: View apps by their relative flow counts.
    - **Total Policy Violations**: View the number of policy violations. For a list of policies, see
      [Policy Availability](https://docs.mulesoft.com/policies/policies-availability-by-gateway#policy-availability).
    - **App Usage: Flow, Message & Request Totals**: Get the flow count, number of messages, and
      request volume for selected apps.
    - **Request Volume by Endpoint (Inbound|Outbound)**: Get the number of inbound and outbound
      requests from your apps. Hovering over the apps provides the number and type of request metric
      (for inbound requests, `mulesoft.app._inbound_.requests.sum`, and for outbound,
      `mulesoft.app._outbound_.requests.sum`).

## Create Custom Dashboards in Tableau Next

Custom dashboards are useful for tailoring your analytics experience to your organizationâs needs.
Use Tableau Next to create custom dashboards for Integration Intelligence. Create visualizations for
your dashboard to show metrics on the MuleSoft telemetry data that matters most to your
organization.

Like Integration Intelligence, Tableau Next is built on the Salesforce Platform.

![Custom Integration Intelligence Dashboard](_images/ii-dashboards-custom.png)

<table><tbody><tr><td><i data-value="1"></i><b>1</b></td><td>Integration Intelligence in the Salesforce Integration app</td></tr><tr><td><i data-value="2"></i><b>2</b></td><td><strong>Dashboard</strong> page in Integration Intelligence</td></tr><tr><td><i data-value="3"></i><b>3</b></td><td>Custom dashboard example in the <strong>My Custom Dashboard</strong> tab</td></tr><tr><td><i data-value="4"></i><b>4</b></td><td>Visualization examples<div><p>Visualizations are visual representations of your data as a chart or graph.</p></div></td></tr><tr><td><i data-value="5"></i><b>5</b></td><td><strong>Manage Dashboard</strong> for access to custom dashboard creation and management features in Tableau Next<div><p>This buttonâs label changes from <strong>Create Dashboard</strong> to <strong>Manage Dashboard</strong> after you provide a dedicated workspace in Tableau Next for custom dashboards in Integration Intelligence.</p></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Before You Begin with Custom Dashboards

Before creating custom dashboards and visualizations for Integration Intelligence in Tableau Next:

- Acquire one of the permission sets associated with the Tableau Next Creator license so that you
  can create a dedicated workspace and manage your custom dashboards. A Salesforce Administrator can
  assign one of these
  [Tableau Next permission sets](https://help.salesforce.com/s/articleView?id=analytics.tua_governance.htm&type=5):
  - Tableau Unmetered Platform Analyst
  - Tableau Next Platform Analyst

- Familiarize yourself with Tableau Next basics. See
  [Learn About Tableau Next](#tableau-next-learn).

### Learn About Tableau Next

If youâre new to Tableau Next, itâs important to understand the basics before creating a custom
dashboard. Tableau Next is a composable AI analytics platform for turning your data into actionable
insights. Use Tableau Next to review, create, and manage your organizationâs assets (workspaces,
data, semantic models, dashboards, and visualizations).

Tableau Next provides in-app resources for learning to work with assets from your organizationâs
home page. To locate these resources:

1.  Navigate to Integration Intelligence dashboards.

    See [Find Dashboards in Integration Intelligence](#nav-ii) for guidance.

2.  From Integration Intelligence, click the ![](_images/ii-app-launcher-icon.png) (App Launcher),
    and then search for and click **Tableau Next**.

    ![App Launcher search for Tableau Next](_images/ii-tableau-next-app-launcher.png)

    For more information, see
    [Tableau Next Home and Workspaces](https://help.salesforce.com/s/articleView?id=analytics.tua_home_workspace.htm&language=en_US&type=5).

3.  Get an introduction to Tableau Next features from the **Getting Started** video and other items
    under **Recommended for You** in your organizationâs home page.
4.  Review your organizationâs assets:
    - Any existing workspaces for collecting and managing analytic assets, launching data analysis,
      and collaborating with your team

      Youâll require a workspace named **Integration Intelligence - Custom Dashboards** for custom
      dashboards in Integration Intelligence.

    - Data, including telemetry data from your Mule apps and APIs, hosted in Data 360 as data lake
      objects
    - Semantic models that map data to standardized terms and logic for useful insights

      Youâll use the **MuleSoft Observability Semantic Model** when creating visualizations for your
      workspace.

    - Visualizations that you can add to your own dashboards

      Visualizations are visual representations of your data as a chart or graph.

For more information, see
[Tableau Next](https://help.salesforce.com/s/articleView?id=analytics.tua.htm&type=5) on the
Salesforce Help site.

After familiarizing yourself with Tableau Next, you can proceed to
[Configure a Workspace for Your Custom Dashboards](#workspace-configure).

### Configure a Workspace for Your Custom Dashboards

Before creating your first custom dashboard for Integration Intelligence, create a workspace named
**Integration Intelligence - Custom Dashboards** and connect to **MuleSoft Observability Semantic
Model**. This model incorporates Anypoint Monitoring metrics to capture insights on your app and API
telemetry data.

1.  Meet the prerequisites in [Before You Begin with Custom Dashboards](#prereqs-custom-dashboards).
2.  From Integration Intelligence, click **Create Dashboard**.

    If **Manage Dashboards** appears instead of **Create Dashboard**, your workspace is already set
    up.

3.  Add a blank workspace named **Integration Intelligence - Custom Dashboards**.

    This required name dedicates the workspace to Integration Intelligence for all custom
    Integration Intelligence dashboards that you create.

    For guidance, See
    [Create a Workspace in Tableau Next](https://help.salesforce.com/s/articleView?id=analytics.tua_workspace_create.htm&type=5).

4.  Add the required semantic model to your workspace:
    1.  Click **Add** and select **Semantic Model** to open **Add Semantic Model**.
    2.  Under **Add Existing**, click **Semantic Model**.
    3.  Select and **Add** the model named **MuleSoft Observability Semantic Model**.

        \+ This model connects to the Integration Intelligence data and metrics required for
        visualizations (such as charts) that you add to your dashboard. Youâre now ready to create a
        custom dashboard for Integration Intelligence. For guidance see
        [Configure a Custom Dashboard in Tableau Next](#dashboard-create).

### Configure a Custom Dashboard in Tableau Next

Use Tableau Next to configure a custom dashboard with visualizations for Integration Intelligence.
You can create one or more dashboards to suit your organizationâs needs, and each dashboard can
contain multiple visualizations.

![Configuration page for a custom dashboard in Tableau Next](_images/ii-dashboards-custom-edit-view.png)

<table><tbody><tr><td><i data-value="1"></i><b>1</b></td><td>A custom dashboard configuration page in Tableau Next<div><p>Notice that the dashboard is in the <strong>Integration Intelligence - Custom Dashboards</strong> workspace so that it can appear in Integration Intelligence.</p></div></td></tr><tr><td><i data-value="2"></i><b>2</b></td><td>A custom dashboard configuration named <strong>My Custom Dashboard</strong></td></tr><tr><td><i data-value="3"></i><b>3</b></td><td>Visualizations in the custom dashboard<div><p>Visualizations are visual representations of your data as a chart or graph.</p></div></td></tr><tr><td><i data-value="4"></i><b>4</b></td><td>Tab for Integration Intelligence workspace (<strong>Integration Intelligence - Custom Dashboards</strong>) in Tableau Next</td></tr><tr><td><i data-value="5"></i><b>5</b></td><td>Tab for a custom dashboard configuration page</td></tr><tr><td><i data-value="6"></i><b>6</b></td><td>Content options for visualizations and metrics, and design options for the workspace (such as grid, theme, and background image)<div><p>For more information, see <a href="https://help.salesforce.com/s/articleView?id=analytics.tua_create_dashboard.htm&amp;type=5">Create Effective Dashboards With Tableau Next</a>.</p></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

Create a custom dashboard:

1.  Meet the prerequisites in [Before You Begin with Custom Dashboards](#prereqs-custom-dashboards).
2.  From Integration Intelligence, click **Manage Dashboards** to open your workspace for your
    custom Integration Intelligence dashboards and related assets.

    If **Create Dashboard** is present instead of **Manage Dashboard**,
    [set up a dedicated workspace in Tableau Next](#workspace-configure) before creating a
    dashboard.

    For more guidance, see the Tableau Next documentation
    [Create a Workspace in Tableau Next](https://help.salesforce.com/s/articleView?id=analytics.tua_workspace_create.htm&type=5).

3.  Add the asset **MuleSoft Observability Semantic Model** to your custom dashboard:

    From the **Add** menu, select **Existing Asset**, and then add the existing MuleSoft model to
    the workspace.

    For more guidance, see
    [Create or Add an Asset in a Workspace](https://help.salesforce.com/s/articleView?id=analytics.tua_workspace_asset.htm&type=5).

4.  Create a custom dashboard with visualizations.

    For guidance, see
    [Create Visualizations and Dashboards in Tableau Next](https://help.salesforce.com/s/articleView?id=analytics.tua_create.htm&language=en_US&type=5).

5.  After creating your custom dashboard and visualization, view the dashboard in Integration
    Intelligence by clicking the tab for your custom dashboard.

    For help opening Integration Intelligence, see
    [Find Dashboards in Integration Intelligence](#nav-ii).

## Copy, Download, or Explore Metrics

Copy or download an image of any chart or table in a dashboard, or open a page dedicated to specific
metrics.

1.  In a chart or table in a dashboard, click an empty area, and then hover to display a
    ![](_images/ii-menu-arrow-icon.png) (menu arrow).
2.  Click ![](_images/ii-menu-arrow-icon.png) (menu arrow).
3.  Select an option:
    - **Copy Image**: Copies a screenshot of the chart or table to your clipboard.
    - **Download Image**: Downloads a screenshot of the chart or table to your local drive.
    - **Explore**: Opens a new tab that contains the selected chart or table on a full page. You can
      also add the chart to an existing dashboard or create a dashboard for the chart from this
      page.

## See Also

- [Tableau Next Learning Resources](https://help.salesforce.com/s/articleView?id=analytics.tua_learning_resources.htm&type=5)
---

# Getting Logs with Integration Intelligence

> For the full documentation index, see: https://docs.mulesoft.com/llms.txt

[![](../_/img/icons/info.svg)](/monitoring/#billing)

Troubleshoot, analyze, and retain logs for your Mule apps and APIs in Integration Intelligence.

![Integration Intelligence Logs](_images/ii-logs.png)

<table><tbody><tr><td><i data-value="1"></i><b>1</b></td><td>Integration Intelligence in the Salesforce Integration app</td></tr><tr><td><i data-value="2"></i><b>2</b></td><td><strong>Logs</strong> in Integration Intelligence</td></tr><tr><td><i data-value="3"></i><b>3</b></td><td>Log count over time</td></tr><tr><td><i data-value="4"></i><b>4</b></td><td>Filters for selecting a <a href="#lookback">time period</a>, specific log levels, loggers, and Mule apps</td></tr><tr><td><i data-value="5"></i><b>5</b></td><td>Logs by timestamp, log level, app, logger, and log message</td></tr></tbody></table>

## Before You Begin

Meet these prerequisites:

Complete the procedures in [Setting Up Integration Intelligence](integration-intelligence-setup).

## Supported Deployment Types

For logs, Integration Intelligence supports Mule apps and Mule APIs deployed to these hosts:

- CloudHub
- CloudHub 2.0
- Runtime Fabric

For dashboards in Integration Intelligence, see
[Supported Deployment Types (for metrics in dashboards)](integration-intelligence-dashboards#supported-deployment-types).

For information about hosts, see
[Runtime Plane Hosting Options](../hosting-home/#runtime-plane-hosting-options).

## Lookback Period

Data collection for logs begins after you set up Integration Intelligence.

From the **Time Period** filter, you can select from predefined ranges for the year (including the
fiscal year), month, week, day (defaults to 30 days), or quarter (including the fiscal quarter), or
you can select a custom date range. The custom date range selector also indicates the available date
range for your data.

## Find Integration Intelligence Logs

Integration Intelligence logs are accessible from your Anypoint Platform and through the Salesforce
App Launcher:

- [Navigate to Logs from Anypoint Platform](#nav-insights)
- [Navigate to Logs from the Salesforce App Launcher](#nav-salesforce)

### Navigate to Logs from Anypoint Platform

Navigate to Integration Intelligence logs from Anypoint Platform.

![Banner for navigating to Integration Intelligence from Anypoint Insights](_images/ii-go-to-ii.png)

1.  Meet the prerequisites in [Before You Begin](#prereqs).
2.  Log in to Anypoint Platform with a user account that has
    [permission to access Anypoint Monitoring content](am-permissions).
3.  Navigate to Anypoint Monitoring:

    Click **Monitoring** from the Anypoint Platform home page or from the **Runtimes** menu in the
    header.

4.  From the banner in the Anypoint Insights header, click **Go to Integration Intelligence**.

    Integration Intelligence automatically opens to **Dashboards**, and **Logs** is accessible from
    the Integration Intelligence sidebar.

5.  From the Integration Intelligence sidebar, click **Logs**.
6.  [Check your logs](#check-logs).

### Navigate to Logs from the Salesforce App Launcher

Integration Intelligence resides in the Integration app. If youâre connected to the Salesforce org
that supports your MuleSoft telemetry data, you can navigate directly to your Integration
Intelligence logs from the App Launcher:

1.  Meet the prerequisites in [Before You Begin](#prereqs).
2.  From your Salesforce environment, click ![](_images/ii-app-launcher-icon.png) (App Launcher).
3.  From the App Launcher, find and select the **Integration** app.

    The launcher opens to the appâs **Intelligence** tab.

4.  From the Integration Intelligence sidebar, click **Logs**.
5.  [Check your logs](#check-logs).

## Check Logs

Use **Logs** in Integration Intelligence to troubleshoot issues.

> [!IMPORTANT] After initial ingestion, data can take up to 10 minutes to appear in Integration
> Intelligence. The first time you open **Logs**, it can take a few additional minutes for events
> and log entries to load.

1.  Navigate to **Logs**.

    For guidance see [Find Integration Intelligence Logs](#nav-ii).

2.  Optionally, narrow the scope of logs:
    - **Log Levels** includes **WARN**, **ERROR**, or **FATAL**.
    - **Logger**: Log4j logger class, such as `HttpListenerRegistry`
    - **Application**: Name of the Mule app that produces the logs.

3.  Review the log message.

    For long messages, you can click the message to display the entire message.
---

# Setting Up Integration Intelligence

> For the full documentation index, see: https://docs.mulesoft.com/llms.txt

[![](../_/img/icons/info.svg)](/monitoring/#billing)

Integration Intelligence enhances monitoring, analysis, and troubleshooting of MuleSoft integrations
through AI-powered capabilities.

## Before You Begin

Before enabling Integration Intelligence in your Anypoint Platform organization, you must have:

- An Anypoint Platform Integration Advanced or legacy Titanium package.
- Salesforce Enterprise, Performance, or Unlimited edition.
- A Tableau Next license.
- Administrative access to an Anypoint Platform organization that isnât a trial organization and
  isnât expired.
- Administrative access to a Salesforce organization.
- [A tenant relationship](../access-management/trusted-salesforce-org) between your Anypoint
  Platform organization and a trusted Salesforce organization established.

  > [!NOTE] The connected Salesforce organization must have Data 360 and Tableau Next enabled. For
  > more information, see
  > [Tableau Next Setup Guide](https://help.tableau.com/current/online/en-us/tableau_next_setup_guide.htm).

- Agentforce enabled in the connected Salesforce organization and your Anypoint Platform
  organization. For more information, see
  [Enabling or Disabling Agentforce for Anypoint Platform](../access-management/enabling-agentforce).
- To avoid installation failures while testing through your Salesforce sandbox organization, make
  sure that your Single source of truth (SSOT) package version is v1.120 or later. For more
  information, see
  [Verify the SSOT Package Version in Data Cloud](https://help.salesforce.com/s/articleView?id=ind.cg_tpm_data_cloud_verify_ssot.htm&type=5).

## Integration Intelligence High-Level Flow

After the Anypoint Platform organization administrator establishes a connection between the Anypoint
Platform organization and the Salesforce organization, the Salesforce admin sets up Integration
Intelligence in the Salesforce organization.

This diagram shows the high-level flow for the Salesforce and Anypoint Platform organization
administrators to set up Integration Intelligence in the Salesforce org.

![Salesforce admin flow for setting up Integration Intelligence](../access-management/_images/ii-salesforce-admin-flow.png)

1.  Log in to your Salesforce organization.
2.  In Salesforce Setup, enter `MuleSoft` in the **Quick Find** box, then select **MuleSoft >
    Integration Intelligence**.
3.  On the **Integration Intelligence Setup Assistant** page, follow the setup steps in order:
    1.  [Set up Data 360](#set-up-data-cloud).
    2.  [Connect with Anypoint Platform](#connect-with-anypoint-platform).
    3.  [Enable Telemetry Data](#enable-telemetry-data).

4.  The Anypoint Platform organization administrator now completes the setup steps to enable
    Integration Intelligence in Anypoint Platform.
5.  After the Anypoint Platform organization administrator completes the setup steps in Anypoint
    Platform, the Salesforce admin completes the rest of the Integration Intelligence setup in the
    Salesforce org.
    1.  [Enable Seamless Login](#enable-seamless-login) for Anypoint Platform users to access
        Tableau Next dashboards in Salesforce.
    2.  [Install Integration Intelligence](#install-integration-intelligence).

## Set up Salesforce Data 360

Connect your Salesforce organization to Data 360. Data 360 is the storage and processing layer for
Integration Intelligence. It ingests MuleSoft telemetry data from your selected business group into
a Data 360 data space.

1.  In the **Data Cloud** section, click **Setup**.

    For information about setting up Data 360, see
    [Get Started with Data 360](https://help.salesforce.com/s/articleView?id=data.c360_a_product_considerations.htm&type=5).

## Complete the Connection with Anypoint Platform

Complete the connection between your Salesforce organization and Anypoint Platform so Salesforce can
ingest MuleSoft telemetry data from your selected business group into Data 360.

1.  From **Setup**, in the Quick Find box, enter `MuleSoft`, and then select **MuleSoft > Anypoint
    Platform Setup**.
2.  On the **MuleSoft Anypoint Platform Setup** page, click **Complete the Connection**.
3.  Copy your Salesforce org tenant key.

    The format for the Salesforce tenant key is `cloud/env/org-id`.

4.  Sign in to Anypoint Platform using an account that has the root Organization Administrator
    permission.
5.  Select **Access Management** from the image::access-management::gear.svg\["gear icon",20\] menu.
6.  Select **Salesforce**.
7.  Click **Add Salesforce Org**.
8.  In **Add the Salesforce Org**, paste the Salesforce org tenant key, and click **Add**.
9.  Enter the name of the Salesforce org in **Name Used in Anypoint Platform**, and click **Add**.

    Anypoint Platform generates and provides the organization key (or tenant key) in the format
    `cloud/env/org-id`.

10. Click **Copy** to copy the Anypoint Platform organization key.
11. Return to the **MuleSoft Anypoint Platform Setup** page in the Salesforce organization.
12. Enter the Anypoint Platform organization key in **Anypoint Platform Organization ID**, and then
    click **Connect**.

    You return to the **MuleSoft Anypoint Platform Setup** page. The page shows the connected
    Anypoint Platform organization details.

## Enable Telemetry Data

Enable telemetry data to stream logs and metrics from your Mule apps and APIs into the selected Data
360 data space.

1.  In Salesforce Setup, in the **Quick Find** box, enter `MuleSoft`, then select **MuleSoft
    Observability**.
2.  In **MuleSoft Observability**, switch **Telemetry Data Ingestion for Integration Intelligence**
    to **Active**.
3.  Return to Anypoint Platform **Access Management > Salesforce**.
4.  In **Capability enablement** for the connected Salesforce organization, switch **You canât bring
    telemetry data into Salesforce Data Cloud** to **Enabled**.
5.  In **Enable Telemetry Data Ingestion**, select the business group that contains the apps that
    will send telemetry data to Data 360, and then click **Next**.

    > [!NOTE] The business group you select for data ingestion canât be changed after you save the
    > settings.

6.  In **Select Applications**, select the environment.

    A list of applications in the selected environment appears.

    > [!NOTE] The application selection list includes specific versions of Mule on CloudHub 2.0 and
    > Runtime Fabric. The list of selectable apps isnât inclusive of all the apps in the selected
    > business groups. For other deployments (CloudHub, Mule Gateway, Omni Gateway), or some earlier
    > versions of Mule, telemetry data ingestion is automatically enabled and excluded from the
    > selection list.

7.  Select the applications to ingest telemetry data for, and click **Enable Data Ingestion**.

    You can search for applications by name and you can also select **Show only applications
    available for selection** to filter the list and show only applications that use the latest
    runtime versions.

    The settings are saved.

    Any subsequent changes made to Integration Intelligence in Access Management are automatically
    shared with the connected Salesforce organization. For example, turning off the Integration
    Intelligence feature in Anypoint Platform disables:
    - Trust with the connected Salesforce organization. Turning it back on restores trust and
      enables data ingestion for the selected business group.
    - Seamless login for Anypoint Platform users to access Tableau Next dashboards in Salesforce.
      Turning it back on restores seamless login.

## Enable Seamless Login for Anypoint Platform Users

The Salesforce admin enables seamless login for Anypoint Platform users to access Tableau Next
dashboards in Salesforce without needing to create new credentials or perform additional login
steps.

1.  If you arenât logged in, log in to Salesforce.
2.  In Salesforce Setup, enter `MuleSoft` in the **Quick Find** box, then select **MuleSoft >
    Anypoint Platform Setup**.
3.  Switch the **Enable Seamless Login from Anypoint Platform** option to **Enabled**.

    > [!NOTE] This option is available after the Anypoint Platform organization administrator
    > completes all their setup steps in Anypoint Platform. If you donât see the option, contact
    > your Anypoint Platform organization administrator.

## Install Integration Intelligence

The Salesforce admin installs Integration Intelligence in the Salesforce org.

> [!NOTE] This step is available only after the Anypoint organization administrator completes all
> the Anypoint Platform setup steps.

1.  In Salesforce Setup, enter `MuleSoft` in the **Quick Find** box, then select **MuleSoft >
    Integration Intelligence Setup Assistant**.
2.  In **Install Integration Intelligence**, click **Setup**.

    The setup steps appear. If a step isnât complete, click **Setup** to complete it. All the steps
    must be completed before you can install Integration Intelligence.

3.  Select **Setup** for **Install Integration Intelligence**.
4.  In **Data Space**, select the data space to use for Integration Intelligence.

    This is the data space where your observability data will be stored and processed and must match
    the data space where telemetry data is ingested.

5.  Click **Next**.
6.  In **App Details**, enter a name and description for the Integration Intelligence app, and then
    click **OK**.

    After the Integration Intelligence app is successfully installed, you see a **Success** status
    and the name of the app is shown on a tab in the **Integration Intelligence Setup** page.

## Next Steps

[Use dashboards](integration-intelligence-dashboards) in the Salesforce ecosystem to monitor Mule
app and API metrics, and get insights into related business data.
---

# Integration Intelligence

> For the full documentation index, see: https://docs.mulesoft.com/llms.txt

[![](../_/img/icons/info.svg)](/monitoring/#billing)

Use Integration Intelligence to extend the value of MuleSoft telemetry beyond real-time monitoring.
Together with Anypoint Monitoring and the Diagnostics Agent, it adds long-term analytics and
actionable insights so that you can maximize uptime and optimize performance over time.

With Integration Intelligence, you can:

- Select a business group for targeted data ingestion so that only the telemetry you need is brought
  into the analytics tier.
- Bring your MuleSoft telemetry data, including metrics, logs, and traces into Salesforce Data 360
  for better data retention and centralized access for analysis.
- Gain long-term performance visibility through deeper data retention in Salesforce Data 360. View
  historical context, trends, seasonality, and recurring issues that real-time monitoring alone
  canât surface, so that you can identify systemic patterns.
- Get actionable insight through dashboards with Tableau Next. Preconfigured pages in the
  Integration Intelligence Lightning App in Salesforce give you a starting point:
  - App Insights, offering a unified view of application metrics, health, and usage.
  - API Insights, providing a unified view of all API metrics, including health and usage.
  - Logs and Traces, enabling you to view tabular pages of log events (including filtering
    capabilities for timeframe and log level). Build custom views that matter to your team for
    operational efficiency, partner performance, and reliability.

- Troubleshoot with agent-assisted analysis to uncover root causes, detect emerging patterns, and
  fine-tune operations without manually combing through logs and dashboards.
- Reach the experience from Anypoint Platform. Cross-platform access with single sign-on (SSO) lets
  Anypoint Platform users log in once to access Tableau Next dashboards in Salesforce, and
  single-click navigation from the Anypoint Monitoring **Insights** page takes you to deeper
  insights.

## Whatâs Next

- Set up Integration Intelligence in a Salesforce organization, and enable Integration Intelligence
  in Anypoint Platform ([Setup](integration-intelligence-setup)).
- Monitor Mule apps and APIs, and get business insights
  ([Dashboards](integration-intelligence-dashboards)).
- Troubleshoot issues reported in your logs ([Logs](integration-intelligence-dashboards)).

## See Also

- [Troubleshooting with MuleSoft Diagnostics Agent](../runtime-manager/troubleshooting#troubleshoot-applications)
---

# Generating Real-Time Logs (US and EU)

> For the full documentation index, see: https://docs.mulesoft.com/llms.txt

[![](../_/img/icons/info.svg)](/monitoring/#billing)

On [US](https://anypoint.mulesoft.com/monitoring) and
[EU](https://eu1.anypoint.mulesoft.com/monitoring) Clouds, the Log Points feature in Anypoint
Monitoring allows you to generate logs for apps and APIs in real-time and without writing code. For
apps, you can specify the level of logs you want to generate, and for APIs, you can specify
properties for the logs. This allows you to extract logs on-demand from applications in real-time,
without causing re-deployment or application downtime. Permissions control allows you to manage
access. All logs seamlessly flow into log management.

## Log Points for Apps

The Application Log Points feature enables you to log information from connectors in any Mule app in
real-time. If you are using one of the supported connectors, you can select a log level (debug,
info, warn, or error) and generate logs.

### Before You Begin

These prerequisites are required:

- A [supported Mule version](../release-notes/mule-runtime/mule-esb).
- The Anypoint Integration Advanced package or a Titanium subscription to Anypoint Platform
- The **Runtime Manager > Manage Settings** permission

### Supported Connectors

The following Anypoint Connectors are available for use with the Log Points feature.

<table><caption>Table 1. Supported Connectors</caption> <colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Supported Anypoint Connectors</th><th>Supported for Mule 4.x</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Amazon S3</p></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Amazon SQS</p></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Database</p></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Email</p></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>File</p></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>FTP</p></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>IBM MQ</p></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>JMS</p></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Microsoft Service Bus</p></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>MongoDB</p></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>MQ</p></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Object Store</p></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Salesforce</p></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>SFTP</p></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Sockets</p></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>VM Transport</p></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Enable Application Log Points

1.  Log in to Anypoint Platform with a user account that has
    [permission to access Anypoint Monitoring content](am-permissions).
2.  Navigate to Anypoint Monitoring:

    Click **Monitoring** from the Anypoint Platform home page or from the **Runtimes** menu in the
    header.

3.  In the sidebar, click **Log Points**.
4.  Select the following:
    - **Environment**: The environment in which the app is running.
    - **Resource name**: The name of the app.

5.  Click **Select**.
6.  Select the connectors for which you want to generate logs.
7.  Select a log level for each connector.
8.  Click **Save & Apply**.  
    A message that your configuration has been applied appears at the top of the page with a **View
    API Logs** link. The logs might take a moment to appear.
9.  Click the **View Logs** link to view the logs for the selected log point.

> [!NOTE] If you select either the trace or debug log level, disable log point monitoring when you
> are finished, to avoid slowing your applicationâs performance.

## Log Points for APIs

The Log Points feature for APIs enables you to interactively extract data from running managed APIs.
You can specify properties of your managed API (for example, the host and method in the request
header) for which to generate logs without having to write code or redeploy your application. This
helps you troubleshoot without having to restart the application.

For example, you can specify a cookie header in an API to see if an injection attack is in progress.
The log data for the specified cookie header is automatically displayed in real time on the Logs
page.

### Prerequisites

To use this feature, you must have the following:

- A [supported Mule version](../release-notes/mule-runtime/mule-esb)
- The Anypoint Integration Advanced package or a Titanium subscription to Anypoint Platform
- The **API Manager > Manage Policies** permission

### Supported APIs

The following types of API are available for use with the Log Points feature:

- Basic endpoint APIs (CloudHub and hybrid)
  - CloudHub
  - Hybrid

- Endpoint with Proxy APIs
  - CloudHub proxy
  - Hybrid proxy

> [!NOTE] All basic endpoint APIs are displayed in the selectors regardless of whether the
> registered API has a supported Mule runtime engine version. If you save a log point for a basic
> endpoint API with an unsupported version, the application policy fails.

## Supported Versions of Mule Runtime Engine

Run your Mule app on a supported Mule runtime version. For supported versions, see
[Mule Runtime Release Notes](../release-notes/mule-runtime/mule-esb).

### Enable API Log Points

1.  Log in to Anypoint Platform with a user account that has
    [permission to access Anypoint Monitoring content](am-permissions).
2.  Navigate to Anypoint Monitoring:

    Click **Monitoring** from the Anypoint Platform home page or from the **Runtimes** menu in the
    header.

3.  In the sidebar, click **Log Points**.
4.  Select the following:
    - **Environment**: The environment the API is running in.
    - **Resource name**: The name of the API.
    - **Version/Instance**: The version and instance of the API.

5.  Click **Select**.
6.  Select the **Request** points to log and monitor.  
    You can monitor the following request header log points:
    - **User Agent**
    - **Path**
    - **Host**
    - **Method**
    - **Remote Access**
    - **Accept**
    - **Accept Encoding**
    - **Accept Language**
    - **Cookie**
    - **Referrer**  
      You can click the blue box to the left of **Header**, to select all log points.  
      Select **Body** to monitor the **Request Body**.

7.  Select the **Response** points to log and monitor.  
    You can monitor the following request header log points:
    - **Status Code**
    - **Connection**
    - **Date**
    - **Set-Cookie**
    - **Pragma**

      You can click the blue box to the left of **Header** to select all log points. Select **Body**
      to monitor the **Response Body**.

8.  Click **Save & Apply**.  
    A message that your configuration has been applied appears at the top of the page with a **View
    API Logs** link. The logs might take a moment to appear.
9.  Click the **View API Logs** link to view the logs for the selected log point.

> [!NOTE] When you are finished monitoring API log points, disable log point monitoring to avoid
> slowing your applicationâs performance.
---

# Log Search Query Syntax Reference (US and EU)

> For the full documentation index, see: https://docs.mulesoft.com/llms.txt

<table><colgroup><col></colgroup><tbody><tr><td><p><span><img src="_images/info.png" alt="info" width="15" height="15" title="This feature requires a Usage-based or Titanium subscription."></span> Availability: <a href="./#billing">Usage-based and Titanium tiers only</a></p></td></tr></tbody></table>

On [US](https://anypoint.mulesoft.com/monitoring) and
[EU](https://eu1.anypoint.mulesoft.com/monitoring) Clouds, fine tune your search by using log query
syntax.

## Search Terms

Search terms consist of terms and operators. Search terms can be either single terms, such as
"hello" or phrases, such as "hello dolly".

You can combine multiple terms with boolean operators to form complex queries.

## Term Modifiers

You can modify query terms using the log search query language to provide a wide range of searching
options.

## Wildcard Searches

You can use single and multiple character wildcard searches within single terms.

> [!NOTE] You canât use wildcard searches within phrase queries.

To perform a single character wildcard search use the `?` symbol. The single character wildcard
search looks for terms that match the single character replaced by `?`. For example, to search for
"text" or "test" you can use the search:

`te?t`

To perform a multiple-character wildcard search, use the \* symbol. Multiple-character wildcard
searches look for zero or more characters. For example, to search for test, tests, or tester, use
the search:

`test*`

You can also use the wildcard searches in the middle of a term, for example: `te*t`.

> [!NOTE] You canât use a \* or `?` symbol as the first character of a search.

## Fuzzy Searches

You can perform fuzzy searches by using the tilde~ symbol at the end of a single word term. For
example, to search for a term similar in spelling to "roam" you can use the fuzzy search:

`roamË·`

This search returns terms like foam and roams.

## Boolean Operators

Boolean operators allow terms to be combined through logic operators. Log search query language
supports `AND`, `+`, `OR`, `NOT` and `-` as Boolean operators.

> [!NOTE] Boolean operators must be ALL CAPS.

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Boolean Operator</th><th>Description</th><th>Example</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><div><div><p><code>AND</code></p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>The <code>AND</code> operator matches documents where both terms exist anywhere in the text of a single document. This is equivalent to an intersection using sets.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>To search for documents that contain "anypoint monitoring" use the query: <code>"anypoint monitoring" AND "results"</code></p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><div><div><p><code>OR</code></p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>The <code>OR</code> operator is used by default if there is no Boolean operator inserted between two terms. The <code>OR</code> operator links two terms and returns a matching document if either of the terms exists in a document. This is equivalent to a union using sets. The symbol <code>| |</code> can be used in place of the word <code>OR</code>.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>To search for documents that contain either "monitoring anypoint" or just "monitoring" use the query:</p></div><div><p><code>"monitoring anypoint" monitoring</code></p></div><div><p>or</p></div><div><p><code>"monitoring anypoint" OR monitoring</code></p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><div><div><p><code>NOT</code></p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>The <code>NOT</code> operator excludes documents that contain the term after <code>NOT</code>. This is equivalent to a difference using sets. The symbol <code>!</code> can be used in place of the word <code>NOT</code>.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>To search for documents that contain "anypoint monitoring" but not "functional", use the query: <code>"anypoint monitoring" NOT "functional"</code><br></p></div><div><div data-type="NOTE">The <code>NOT</code> operator canât be used with just one term. For example, the following search returns no results:<br><code>NOT "functional"</code></div></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><div><div><p><code>+</code></p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Requires that the term after the <code>+</code> symbol exists somewhere in the field of a single document.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>To search for documents that must contain "anypoint" and may contain "monitoring" use the query:</p></div><div><p><code>+anypoint monitoring</code></p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><div><div><p><code>-</code></p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Excludes documents that contain the term after the <code>-</code> symbol.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>To search for documents that contain "anypoint monitoring" but not "results" use the query:<br></p></div><div><p><code>"anypoint monitoring" -"results"</code></p></div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

## Special Characters

You can escape special characters that are part of the query syntax. The current list of supported
special characters includes:

`+ - && || ! ( ) { } [ ] ^ " ~ * ? : \ < >`

To escape these characters, use the `\` before the character. For example, to search for (1+1):2 use
the query:

`\(1\+1\)\:2`

## Search Fields

Specify a field name or use the default when performing a search. The field name and default field
are specific to the search implementation.

Search any field by typing the field name, followed by a colon, and then the term you are looking
for. For example, to find logs with a message that contains "called the function Value Selector" in
the app "my-app", enter either of the following:

- `appId: "my-app" AND message: "called the function Value Selector"`
- `appId: "my-app" AND "called the function Value Selector"`

The default field, `message`, does not require a field indicator in the query.
---

# Using Logs in Anypoint Monitoring (Canada and Japan)

> For the full documentation index, see: https://docs.mulesoft.com/llms.txt

**Open Beta Release**: In the Canada and Japan Clouds, Logs and Alerts features are in open beta.
Any use of Anypoint Monitoring features in the beta state is subject to the applicable beta services
terms and conditions.

<table><colgroup><col></colgroup><tbody><tr><td><p><span><img src="_images/info.png" alt="info" width="15" height="15" title="This feature requires an Advanced subscription in a supported Anypoint Platform region."></span> Availability: <a href="./#billing">Advanced tier only</a></p></td></tr></tbody></table>

In the [Canada](https://ca1.platform.mulesoft.com/monitoring) and
[Japan](https://jp1.platform.mulesoft.com/monitoring) Clouds, search for up to 30 days of logs
across apps deployed to one of your organizationâs environments.

![Logs UI](_images/logs-hyperforce.png)

<table><tbody><tr><td><i data-value="1"></i><b>1</b></td><td>Logs in Anypoint Monitoring</td></tr><tr><td><i data-value="2"></i><b>2</b></td><td><a href="#environments">Environment selector</a> for your deployments (such as Sandbox or Production)</td></tr><tr><td><i data-value="3"></i><b>3</b></td><td><a href="#ref-query">Filters</a> to narrow the list of logs that return</td></tr><tr><td><i data-value="4"></i><b>4</b></td><td>Matching log entry, which includes a date-time stamp, log level, and message</td></tr><tr><td><i data-value="5"></i><b>5</b></td><td>Expanded log message data and data available from other log fields</td></tr><tr><td><i data-value="6"></i><b>6</b></td><td>Selector for the date and time range to capture</td></tr><tr><td><i data-value="7"></i><b>7</b></td><td><a href="#refresh">Refresh</a> button</td></tr></tbody></table>

To view logs in other Anypoint Platform clouds, see
[Using Logs in Anypoint Monitoring (US and EU)](logs-us-eu). To view single-application logs in
Runtime Manager, see [View Log Data](../cloudhub/viewing-log-data).

## Search for Logs

1.  Select a date-time range (defaults to the last 15 minutes).

    Built-in and custom selections are available for up to the last 30 days of logs for selected
    time ranges.

2.  Select an environment.
3.  To narrow the search results, provide a [log query](#ref-query).
4.  Review the search results.

    You can expand a log entry to get additional details.

    If no logs return, try a different date-time range, query, or environment. Also review the
    [search query behavior](#search-query-rules).

## View the Latest Logs

After a search query executes and produces log data (or no results), no new log data appears until
you perform a new query, refresh, or reload the page.

To retrieve the latest log data, click **Refresh**.

## Environments

When displaying logs, select the environment containing the applications you want to review.

Your organization administrator
[creates](../access-management/environments#to-create-a-new-environment) the environments that are
available to you.

Anypoint Platform supports these _types_ of
[environments](../access-management/environments#types-of-environments.adoc):

- **Design**
- **Production**
- **Sandbox**

## Log Query Reference

Use these filters to narrow the results of your search for logs:

- `application` accepts an **\=** operator followed by an application name.
- `log_level` accepts an **\=** operator followed by a _case-sensitive_ value of **DEBUG**,
  **INFO**, **WARN**, **ERROR**, or **FATAL**.
- `message` accepts a **LIKE** operator for partial matches in log `message` fields.

Search query behavior:

- You can use any or all of the available filters in your query.

  Multiple filters in a query are processed as logical ANDs (not ORs), meaning results must match
  all filter criteria not just some.

- Multiple filters of the same type, such as two `application` filters, arenât allowed.
- Each filter allows a single value.
- For single-word search string values, you donât need to add quotation marks around the string. For
  multi-word strings, manually surround the value in _single_ quotes, such as `'error request'`.
---

# Using Enhanced Log Search in Anypoint Monitoring (US and EU)

> For the full documentation index, see: https://docs.mulesoft.com/llms.txt

[![](../_/img/icons/info.svg)](/monitoring/#billing)

From May through June 2026, Anypoint Monitoring is rolling out an enhanced Log Search experience in
US and EU instances of Anypoint Platform. This version replaces the
[traditional Log Search](logs-us-eu).

Get logs on your Mule apps from Anypoint Monitoring. On
[US](https://anypoint.mulesoft.com/monitoring) and
[EU](https://eu1.anypoint.mulesoft.com/monitoring) Clouds, Anypoint Monitoring aggregates log files
so you can manage, search for, filter, and analyze your logs. These features support:

- Comparative analysis and pattern identification across Mule apps
- Focused log searches to isolate key data
- Sharing log searches and CSV downloads

![Log Search UI](_images/logs-search-hf.png)

<table><tbody><tr><td><i data-value="1"></i><b>1</b></td><td>Search for log field columns to display or remove from the log <strong>Results</strong> table. Hover over a field name to add or remove the column, and to display top-5 values from the field.<div><ul><li><p><strong>Selected fields</strong>: Lists columns added to the log <strong>Results</strong> table</p></li><li><p><strong>Available fields</strong>: Lists columns you can add to the log <strong>Results</strong> table</p></li></ul></div></td></tr><tr><td><i data-value="2"></i><b>2</b></td><td>Provide search queries for logs and log fields, including log <strong>Message</strong> fields. Use <span><img src="_images/logs-save-icon-hf.png" alt="" width="20" height="20"></span> (Save) to save named searches for future use, and to select, update, or delete saved searches.</td></tr><tr><td><i data-value="3"></i><b>3</b></td><td>Set a date and time range to capture in the log <strong>Results</strong> table using the clickable <span><img src="_images/logs-calandar-icon-hf.png" alt="" width="20" height="20"></span> (Calendar), <strong>Show dates</strong>, or a date range that appears in the UI after you click <strong>Show dates</strong>. If you select a relative or absolute date range, the <strong>Refresh</strong> button switches to an <strong>Update</strong> button to use for applying your date range.</td></tr><tr><td><i data-value="4"></i><b>4</b></td><td><strong>Refresh</strong> is for manually refreshing the logs in the <strong>Results</strong> table.<div><p>To set an autorefresh rate in seconds, minutes, or hours, open <span><img src="_images/logs-calandar-icon-hf.png" alt="" width="20" height="20"></span> (Calendar) to configure the <strong>Refresh every</strong> setting.</p></div></td></tr><tr><td><i data-value="5"></i><b>5</b></td><td><strong>Share</strong> provides a link to your current search query.</td></tr><tr><td><i data-value="6"></i><b>6</b></td><td>Create and use <a href="#search-filters">search filters</a>, such as <code>appId: app</code>. Filters <a href="#save-search">persist for reuse only if you save them</a>.</td></tr><tr><td><i data-value="7"></i><b>7</b></td><td>Review log counts by the selected date range. Timestamp units on the horizontal axis vary depending on the date or time range you select. The longer the range, the longer the timestamp period, which can display averages over 30-second, 12-hour, or weekly intervals, for example.</td></tr><tr><td><i data-value="8"></i><b>8</b></td><td>Use the <strong>Results</strong> table to review the logs captured by your search. You can also use <strong>Download as CSV</strong> to download up to 10,000 rows of log data.</td></tr><tr><td><i data-value="9"></i><b>9</b></td><td>Filter by the value in a cell of the <strong>Results</strong> table by hovering over the cell and clicking to add (<strong>+</strong>) or remove (<strong>-</strong>) the filter.</td></tr></tbody></table>

> [!NOTE]
>
> - For agent-related logs, see [Get Logs for Agentic Assets](#search-agentic).
> - For on-premises servers with customized logging, see important information in
>   [Format Customized Logs in On-Premises Servers for Proper Indexing](#warning-custom-logs).

## Search for Logs

Use **Log Search** to capture the logs you want to review. You can use the search bar, a filter
(including filters created with Query DSL), or combination of the search bar and a filter.

- [Query Logs from the Search Bar](#search-bar)
- [Use Search Filters](#search-filters)

### Query Logs from the Search Bar

Use the search bar with or without [search filters](#search-filters) to capture the logs you want to
review.

1.  Select an absolute or relative date-time range to review.

    For date-time range selection, see [Select a Log Date and Time Range](#time-range).

2.  Optionally, add or remove columns (available fields) from the log **Results** table.
3.  Provide a search query using any of these methods:
    - Provide a query in the search bar:
      - Use words or quotes around multiple words and strings containing special characters, such as
        `"HTTP GET"`, `"ab12c345-abc6-789d-e1fa-b124c5de6789"`, or
        `"https://api.mycompany.com:443"`.

        Without quotes, multiword searches return matches to each word, and unescaped special
        characters can affect the search negatively. For partial word searches, you can use pattern
        matching characters in your search, such as `mya*`, or `appId: mya?p`.

      - Escape special characters with the `\` character.

        For more information, see [Special Characters](log-search-query-syntax#special-characters).

      - Use a [field identifier](#field-names) to search within a specific log field, such as
        `appId: myapp` for an application named `myapp`.
      - Use logical operators, such as the `AND` in `appId: myap* AND "forbidden (403)"`.

        For a list of supported operators, see
        [Boolean Operators](log-search-query-syntax#boolean-operators).

    - Add filters as needed to change the scope of log results.

      For guidance, see [Use Search Filters](#search-filters).

4.  Review search results in the **Results** table:

![Expanded Log Message](_images/logs-message-expanded-hf.png)

<table><tbody><tr><td><i data-value="1"></i><b>1</b></td><td>To expand a log entry and view log fields, click <strong>&gt;</strong> in the <strong>Time</strong> field for a log entry.<div><p>The arrowhead points down (<strong>â</strong>) after you click it.</p></div></td></tr><tr><td><i data-value="2"></i><b>2</b></td><td>The expanded log (<strong>Expanded document</strong>) includes <strong>Table</strong> and <strong>JSON</strong> views.</td></tr><tr><td><i data-value="3"></i><b>3</b></td><td>For long, truncated log messages, open the full message by clicking the <strong>&gt;</strong> in the <strong>Table</strong> viewâs <strong>Message</strong> field.</td></tr><tr><td><i data-value="4"></i><b>4</b></td><td>Get more context on the log by clicking <strong>View surrounding logs</strong> and specifying a number of logs that precede and follow your log, or use <strong>View log</strong> to open the log entry in a dedicated page.</td></tr></tbody></table>

For more detail, see [Log Search Query Syntax Reference (US and EU)](log-search-query-syntax).

### Use Search Filters

Create, edit, and apply log search filters. When defining a filter, you can use selectable fields
and operators, or you can use the JSON-based OpenSearch Query Domain-Specific Language (Query DSL)
for more complex filters.

- [Create a Basic Filter](#filter-simple)
- [Create a Filter with Query DSL](#filter-query-dsl)
- [Use Top 5 Log Field Values to Create a Filter](#filter-top5)

To save a filter for future use, see [Save and Apply Saved Log Search Queries](#save-search).

#### Create a Basic Filter
