Create a filter for your log search:

1.  Click **Add filter**.
2.  Select a log field name, or use the fieldâs identifier (such as `appId`) for Query DSL filters.

    See [Log Field Names](#field-names) for log fields and identifiers.

3.  Select an operator, and provide any required operands:
    - `is`: Contains the value you provide
      - Example: `Application is my-app`
      - Filter label: `appId: my-app`

    - `is not`: Doesnât contain the value you provide
      - Example: `Application is not my-app`
      - Filter label: `NOT appId: my-app`

    - `is one of`: Matches at least one value in the list of values you provide
      - Example: `Application is one of my-app another-app yet-another-app`
      - Filter label: `appId: is one of my-app another-app yet-another-app`

    - `is not one of`: Doesnât match any value in the list of values you provide
      - Example: `Application is not one of my-app another-app yet-another-app`
      - Filter label: `NOT appId: is one of my-app another-app yet-another-app`

    - `exists`: Identifies a field that contains a value
      - Example: `Class exists`
      - Filter label: `class: exists`

    - `does not exist`: Identifies a field that contains an empty value
      - Example: `Class does not exist`
      - Filter label: `NOT class: exists`

4.  Optionally, perform any of these actions:
    - When creating or editing a search filter, use **Create custom label?**.

      A short label is useful for long filter names.

    - Save your filter so you can reuse it.

      For guidance, see [Save and Apply Saved Log Search Queries](#save-search).

#### Create a Filter with Query DSL

You can use OpenSearch Query Domain-Specific Language (Query DSL) to create a filter in JSON format.
For information about valid log field identifiers, see [Log Field Names](#field-names).

1.  Click **Add a filter**.
2.  Click **Edit Query DSL**.
3.  Provide your query, and click **Save**.

    This example shows a query for `log-level` INFO:

    ```text
    {
      "query": {
        "match": {
          "log-level": {
            "query": "INFO",
            "type": "phrase"
          }
        }
      }
    }
    ```

    This example shows a complex query that retrieves worker ID values from 0 to 20.

{ "query": { "range": { "workerId": { "gte": 0, "lte": 20 } } } }

To learn more, see [Query DSL](https://docs.opensearch.org/3.3/query-dsl/) in the v3.3 OpenSearch
documentation.

#### Use Top 5 Log Field Values to Create a Filter

Get a list of top-5 log values from the list of fields used for columns in the **Results** table.
For example, you can get a list of the top-5 apps or log messages ranked by the percentage of
matches, such as 19% of log messages that match.

![Top 5 Application Fields Example](_images/logs-top5-hf.png)

1.  Hover over a field, such as **Application**, from the list of log field names.
2.  Click ![](_images/logs-top5-icon-hf.png) (Top 5) to open the list of top 5 values for the field.
3.  Click **+** (Add) to create a search filter for this value.

    You can also negate the top-5 value filter by clicking the **\-**. For example, you can use
    these features to create a filter on a log-level value (**log-level: ERROR**) or negate the
    value (**NOT log-level: ERROR**).

4.  Optionally, save the search filter for reuse.

    For guidance, see [Save and Apply Saved Log Search Queries](#save-search).

### Select a Log Date and Time Range

By default Log Search retrieves the last 15 minutes of logs. You can use an absolute or relative
date-time range:

- **Absolute**: Specific date-time to start and end the search. The end of the date-time period
  defaults to **now**.
- **Relative**: A configurable number of seconds, minutes, hours, days, weeks, months, or years
  before (such as **Days ago**) or after (such as **Days from now**) the current date and time.

To select the date-time range, use any of these options:

- Click ![](_images/logs-calandar-icon-hf.png) (Calendar), and use **Quick select** or **Commonly
  used** options:
  - Select the last number of **seconds**, **minutes**, **hours**, **days**, **weeks**, **months**,
    or **years**.
  - Select a commonly used option, such as **Today**, **This week**, last number of minutes, hours,
    or days, **Last 1 year**.

- Click **Show dates** or the date-time range in Log Search:
  - Click **Absolute** to pick a specific start date and time for your log search, and then click
    **Update**.
  - Click **Relative**, and set a number of seconds, minutes, hours, days, weeks, months, or years
    from now for your log search, and then click **Update**.

    Optionally, round to the day.

For information about log data retention, see
[Data Retention and Management for Logs](#data-retention-management).

## Save and Apply Saved Log Search Queries

Save search queries as reusable filters. After using a saved search query, you can clear the query
filter so it doesnât affect search results. You can also permanently delete a saved search.

- To save a new search:
  1.  Run a search query you intend to save.

      For guidance, see [Search for Logs](#search-logs).

  2.  Click ![](_images/logs-save-icon-hf.png) (Save).
  3.  Click **Save as new** or **Save current search**.

      **Save as new** appears if another filter or search query is applied to the current log
      search.

  4.  Provide a name for your search, and click **Save**.

      Wait a moment for the save process to complete.

- To use a saved search:
  1.  Click ![](_images/logs-save-icon-hf.png) (Save).
  2.  Click the saved search to apply the search.

      The search appears as a search filter.

- To clear a saved search from a search query thatâs filtering your results, use either of these
  methods:
  - Click ![](_images/logs-save-icon-hf.png) (Save), hover over the saved search, and then click
    **Clear**.
  - Hover over the filter and click **X**. For example, if **my saved app** is a saved search, you
    can click **X** to clear it without deleting:

    ![Delete a Filter](_images/logs-delete-filter-hf.png)

    The saved filter remains available in your list of saved searches. Unsaved search filters donât
    persist when you click **X**.

- To edit a saved search:
  1.  Click a saved search to display the results.
  2.  Modify your current search query or filters.
  3.  Click ![](_images/logs-save-icon-hf.png) (Save).
  4.  Click **Save changes** to modify your saved search.
  5.  Click **Save**.

      Wait a moment for the save process to complete.

- To delete a saved search:
  1.  Click ![](_images/logs-save-icon-hf.png) (Save).
  2.  Hover over the saved search to delete.
  3.  Click ![](_images/logs-delete-icon-hf.png) (Delete).

## Move and Remove Columns in the Results

After adding optional columns to the log **Results** table, such as **Application** and **Log
level**, you can move and remove them.

Hover over the column heading, such as **Log level**, to reveal column options:

- To move a column, use the arrow to move the column to the left or right.
- To remove a column, click the **X**.
- To sort the data in the column in ascending or descending order, click its up-down arrow to switch
  the sorting order.

## Refresh Log Data (Manually and Automatically)

You can refresh log data manually and autorefresh on a regular basis:

- To refresh manually, click **Refresh**.
- To autorefresh on a regular basis:
  1.  Click ![](_images/logs-calandar-icon-hf.png) (Calendar).
  2.  Under **Refresh every**, provide a number and time unit (seconds, minutes, or hours), such as
      **5 minutes**.
  3.  Click **Start** to initiate the autorefresh process.

## Download Log Results as a CSV File

Download log search results for up to 10,000 log records into a CSV file.

1.  Add columns for log fields you want in your report to the log **Results** table.

    For guidance, see [Move and Remove Columns in the Results](#adjust-columns).

2.  Run your search query.

    For guidance, see [Search for Logs](#search-logs).

3.  Click **Download as CSV**.

## Get Logs for Agentic Assets

API Manager and Agent Visualizer provide links to logs for agent-related assets (agent, broker, and
MCP server instances):

- In Agent Visualizer, click a node in the visual graph, and navigate to logs. For guidance, see
  [Access Logs](../agent-visualizer/troubleshoot-agent-network#access-logs) in the Agent Visualizer
  documentation.

  For more information about Agent Visualizer, see
  [Agent Visualizer Overview](../agent-visualizer/).

- In API Manager, navigate to **Agent and Tool Instances**, and click an instance:
  - To find logs for policies applied to agents, brokers, and MCP servers, click **Message Log**,
    and then click **View logs in Anypoint Monitoring**.
  - For a brokerâs reasoning logs, click **Message Log**, and then click the button dedicated to the
    broker. These logs come from the broker application.

    > [!IMPORTANT] By default, broker logs include the actions the broker takes (including agents
    > called and MCP tools executed), but they donât include reasoning data due to potential logging
    > of personally identifiable information.

    If your organization opts to enable a brokerâs reasoning logs, set the brokerâs `category` field
    in Runtime Manager:

    `INSECURE-LOGGING` category to `DEBUG`

## Format Customized Logs in On-Premises Servers for Proper Indexing

Logs produced by apps with customized logging (by changing the pattern layout in the `log4j2.xml`
file) can fail to index correctly into Anypoint Monitoring. To ensure correct indexing, use the Mule
default pattern layout, which is one of the following:

- `<PatternLayout pattern="%-5p %d [%t] [event: %X{correlationId}] %c: %m%n" />`, or
- `<PatternLayout pattern="%d [%t] %-5p %c - %m%n" />`

For applications deployed to CloudHub and Anypoint Runtime Fabric with customized time zones:

Donât override the default JVM time zone for applications deployed to CloudHub and Anypoint Runtime
Fabric with customized time zones. The time zone must be UTC. Changing the time zone to a local time
zone causes logs to be indexed in Anypoint Monitoring with incorrect timestamps.

## Data Retention and Management for Logs

Log storage limits for your subscription tier determine your retention and arenât based on a set
amount of time. For details, see
[Data Retention Limits](performance-and-impact#data-retention-limits).

For other limitations on logs, see
[Logging Data Management](performance-and-impact#logging-data-management).

## Reference

Get descriptions of fields and filter options:

- [Log Field Names](#field-names): Log field names and identifiers
- [Filter Options](#filter-options): Options for enabling, disabling, inverting, including,
  excluding, editing, and deleting filters

### Log Field Names

Log field names in the UI have field identifiers that appear in filter labels and
[saved search labels](#save-search). [Query DSL filters](#filter-query-dsl) require field
identifiers.

- **Application** (`appId`): Name of the application
- **Class** (`class`): Class name
- **Environment** (`envId`): Environment identifier
- **Event** (`event`): Identifier for the associated Mule event
- **Log level** (`log-level`): Log value such as INFO, WARN, ERROR
- **Logger** (`logger`): Name of the logger
- **Message** (`message`): Log message
- **Timestamp** (`timestamp`): Date and time of a log entry
- **Worker** (`workerId`): Identifier for the associated worker

### Filter Options

Filter options are available globally and to individual filters.

![Filter Icons](_images/filter-icons-hf.png)

<table><tbody><tr><td><i data-value="1"></i><b>1</b></td><td>Click the filter icon to open global filter options:<div><ul><li><p><strong>Enable all</strong> to enable all your filters</p></li><li><p><strong>Disable all</strong> to disable all your filters without deleting the filters</p></li><li><p><strong>Invert inclusion</strong> to convert filters that include matching results (such as <code>log-level: ERROR</code>) into filters that uses <code>NOT</code> to exclude matching result (such as <code>NOT log-level: ERROR</code>) and to convert filters that use <code>NOT</code> to exclude matching results into filters that include matching results</p></li><li><p><strong>Invert enabled/disabled</strong> to disable all enabled filters or to enable all disabled filters</p></li></ul></div></td></tr><tr><td><i data-value="2"></i><b>2</b></td><td>Click the filter label to open individual filter options:<div><ul><li><p><strong>Edit filter</strong> to change the filter configuration</p></li><li><p><strong>Include results</strong> to include the filterâs results in the <strong>Results</strong> table</p></li><li><p><strong>Exclude results</strong> to apply the <strong>NOT</strong> operator to the filter, which excludes its results from the <strong>Results</strong> table</p></li><li><p><strong>Temporarily disable</strong> to retain the filter without using it to return search results; switches to <strong>Re-enable</strong> when you click <strong>Temporarily disable</strong></p></li><li><p><strong>Delete</strong> to permanently delete the filter</p></li></ul></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

Any options related to pinning or unpinning filters arenât used.
---

# Using Logs in Anypoint Monitoring (US and EU)

> For the full documentation index, see: https://docs.mulesoft.com/llms.txt

[![](../_/img/icons/info.svg)](/monitoring/#billing)

From May through June 2026, Anypoint Monitoring is rolling out an
[enhanced Log Search experience](logs-search-hf) in US and EU instances of Anypoint Platform. This
version replaces the traditional Log Search.

On [US](https://anypoint.mulesoft.com/monitoring) and
[EU](https://eu1.anypoint.mulesoft.com/monitoring) Clouds, Anypoint Monitoring aggregates log files
so you can manage, search for, filter, and analyze your logs. You can use aggregated logs in
conjunction with monitoring to help identify issues and investigate failures quickly. These features
support:

- Comparative analysis and identification of event and information patterns based on searches across
  selected Mule apps and environments.
- Searches within the logs of a specific worker (or set of workers) for a Mule app so that you can
  narrow scope of issues to check.
- Sharing a snapshot of important logs with developers who can work on the source of the issue.

You can also search for individual application logs in Runtime Manager, see
[View Log Data](../cloudhub/viewing-log-data).

> [!CAUTION] **For on-premises servers with customized logging:**
>
> Logs produced by apps with customized logging (by changing the pattern layout in the `log4j2.xml`
> file) might not get indexed correctly into Anypoint Monitoring. To ensure correct indexing, use
> the Mule default pattern layout, which is one of the following:
>
> - `<PatternLayout pattern="%-5p %d [%t] [event: %X{correlationId}] %c: %m%n" />`, or
> - `<PatternLayout pattern="%d [%t] %-5p %c - %m%n" />`
>
> **For applications deployed to CloudHub and Anypoint Runtime Fabric with customized time zones:**
>
> Do not override the default JVM timezone for applications deployed to CloudHub and Anypoint
> Runtime Fabric with customized time zones. The timezone must be UTC. Changing the timezone to a
> local timezone causes logs to be indexed in Anypoint Monitoring with incorrect timestamps.

## Search Logs

In the sidebar, click **Log Search** to search for text in logs from the search field, through a
filter, or with a DSL query.

![Logs in Anypoint Monitoring](_images/intro-logs.png)

- [Search from the Log Search Field](#search-field)
- [Search with a Filter](#search-filter)
- [Search with the Query DSL](#search-dsl)
- [Search for agent, broker, or MCP server logs](#search-agentic)

When you search for logs for APIs deployed through Omni Gateway (formerly Flex Gateway), use the
**Application** filter. Use the **App ID** field to identify the instance.

### Search from the Log Search Field

To search the logs, type your search query into the log search field and press **Enter**.

- Search for a term, such as `"Info Dynamic Logging"`.

  Without quotes, the query returns results for matches to each term.

- Use a wildcard in your search, such as `appId=myap*`.
- Use logical operators, such as `appId=myap* AND "Info Dynamic Logging"`.

### Search with a Filter

You can create a filter to search logs using predefined fields, operators, and values.

1.  Click **+Add a filter**.
2.  Select the predefined field from the drop-down list.
    - `application`
    - `class`
    - `environment`
    - `environment type`
    - `log level`
    - `logger`
    - `message`
    - `thread`
    - `timestamp`
    - `worker`

3.  Select the operator from the drop-down list.

    Operators vary by the field you select.

4.  If applicable, enter a value, for example:

    ![A form to add a filter for errors](_images/log-filter-example.png)

    See [Search Filters](#filters_predefined) and [Operators](#operators).

5.  (Optional) Enter a value in **Label**.
6.  Click **Save**.

    NOTE

    Results for the **Application** filter include APIs deployed through Omni Gateway (formerly Flex
    Gateway).

### Search with the Query DSL

To convert a search query into a Query DSL:

1.  Click **+Add a filter**.
2.  Click **Edit Query DSL**.
3.  Enter your query, and click **Save**.  
    This example shows a query for log-level INFO:

    ```text
    {
      "query": {
        "match": {
          "log-level": {
            "query": "INFO",
            "type": "phrase"
          }
        }
      }
    }
    ```

    The search query UI provides a link to the query documentation so that you can perform more
    complex queries than otherwise available.

    This example shows a complex query.

{ "query": { "range": { "workerId": { "gte": 0, "lte": 20 } } } }

> [!NOTE] Some complex search queries do not have an equivalent in the search filter values UI, so
> you can only create and view such queries in the query UI.

## View Log Data

By default, the log entries in your log search results are abbreviated. You can expand and view the
log message and details as a table or in JSON format.

![A log file entry](_images/log-expanded.png)

### Tabular Logs

This example shows an expanded log entry as a table. The table contains log filter fields that you
see in the Quick Filter list.

```text
application	      abcd40x.us-a1.abc.cloudhub.io
class	            qtp437897409-31
environment	      Sandbox
environment type	%{[fields][env_type]}
log level	        ERROR
logger            DefaultMessagingExceptionStrategy
message
                  ****************************************************
                  Message               : No record could be found in payload or in flow variable BATCH_RECORD (java.lang.IllegalStateException).
                  Element               : null
                  ----------------------------------------------------
                  Exception stack is:
                  No record could be found in payload or in flow variable BATCH_RECORD (java.lang.IllegalStateException). (org.mule.api.transformer....
                  (72 more...)

                  (set debug level logging or '-Dmule.verbose.exceptions=true' for everything)
                  ****************************************************
timestamp         April 25th 2024, 17:09:53.517
worker            0
```

### JSON-Formatted Logs

This example shows an expanded log entry in JSON format.

```json
{
  "_version": 1,
  "_source": {
    "class": "qtp437897409-31",
    "logger": "DefaultMessagingExceptionStrategy",
    "_ending": 0,
    "timestamp": "2024-11-26T00:09:53.517Z",
    "message": ".Example***
    .ExampleMessage: No record could be found in payload or in flow variable
    BATCH_RECORD (java.lang.IllegalStateException)..ExampleElement...",
    "log level": "ERROR",
    "worker": "0",
    "environment": "Sandbox",
    "environment type": "sandbox",
    "application": "test40x.us-e1.qax.cloudhub.io"
  },
  "fields": {
    "timestamp": [
      "2024-11-26T00:09:53.517Z"
    ]
  },
  "highlight": {
    "orgId": [
      "@abcde@6046b96d@/..."
    ]
  },
  "sort": [
    1524701393517
  ]
}
```

## Get Logs for Agentic Assets

API Manager and Agent Visualizer provide links to logs for agentic assets (agent, broker, and MCP
server instances):

- In Agent Visualizer, click a node in the visual graph, and navigate to logs. For guidance, see
  [Access Logs](../agent-visualizer/troubleshoot-agent-network#access-logs) in the Agent Visualizer
  documentation.

  For more information about Agent Visualizer, see
  [Agent Visualizer Overview](../agent-visualizer/).

- In API Manager, navigate to **Agent and Tool Instances**, and click an instance:
  - To find logs for policies applied to agents, brokers, and MCP servers, click **Message Log**,
    and then click **View logs in Anypoint Monitoring**.
  - For a brokerâs reasoning logs, click **Message Log**, and then click the button dedicated to the
    broker. These logs come from the broker application.

    > [!IMPORTANT] By default, broker logs include the actions the broker takes (including agents
    > called and MCP tools executed), but they donât include reasoning data due to potential logging
    > of personally identifiable information.

    If your organization opts to enable a brokerâs reasoning logs, set the brokerâs `category` field
    in Runtime Manager:

    `INSECURE-LOGGING` category to `DEBUG`

### View Full Logs on a Dedicated Page

After finding a log on a particular issue, you can view the complete log on a dedicated page
(without the quick filters, search field, and filter tags). You can view the log detail in tabular
or JSON format.

1.  From the selected log, click **View This Log**.

    ![View This Log feature](_images/logs-view-surrounding.png)

2.  View the extended log in tabular or JSON format.

    ![Extended logs example](_images/logs-view-extended.png)

## View Surrounding Logs

After finding a log for a particular issue, you might want to know what other log reports preceded
or followed it.

1.  From the selected log, click **View Surrounding Logs**.

    ![View Surrounding Logs tab](_images/logs-view-surrounding.png)

2.  Load the number of logs that you want to see before and after the selected log, or use the
    default.

    ![Surrounding logs view example](_images/logs-set-surrounding.png)

The default settings are:

- **Load 5 more** newer logs
- **Load 5 more** older logs

## Working with Predefined Filters

When the **Quick Filters** list is open, you can select and use predefined log filters.

> [!NOTE] For every result group, the **Quick Filters** list displays the first five results of the
> most recent 500 log entries. When there are more than 500 entries or the top five values do not
> represent 100% of total values, a **See More** link for each result group appears. You can click
> **See More** to open the **Add a Filter** box and set parameters to further filter your results.

This example of a predefined filter selects the ERROR log level.

![Predefined log filter](_images/log-filter-select.png)

After you select a predefined filter from the Quick Filter list, you see it near the top of the
page, next to **Add a Filter**.

![Predefined filter actions](_images/log-filter-actions.png)

When you hover over a filter, you can select from the following actions:

- **Enable/Disable** - Enables or disables the filter. When the filter is disabled, it is grayed
  out.
- **Pin/Unpin** - Pin or unpin the filter.
- **Invert** - Change the operation of the search filter. For example, if the search is
  `log level IS ERROR`, the inversion is `log level IS NOT ERROR`.
- **Remove** - Delete the filter.
- **Edit** - Change the filter configuration.

### Adding Filter Columns to Logs

From the Quick Filters list, you can add and remove columns to or from log results. For example, you
might add the Log Level, Timestamp, or Environment field.

Click the column icon for a filter field, for example:

![Log filter column example](_images/log-filter-add-field.png)

The resulting `log-level` column looks like this in the logs:

![Filter column in the logs](_images/log-filter-column.png)

To remove the filter column, click the column icon for the filter field, for example:

![Column icon for removing the column from the logs](_images/log-filter-remove-field.png)

You can also remove it with the [X icon](#column_move) that appears when you scroll over the column.

## Moving Filter Columns

To move filter columns in your log results to the left or right, you simply scroll over the column
heading and use the arrow icons.

![Arrow icons for moving a filter column to the left or right](_images/log-filter-column-move.png)

### Search Filters

Use predefined filters or filters that you create.

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Filter</th><th>Description</th><th>Examples</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><code>application</code></p></td><td><p>Full domain of the Mule app in CloudHub.</p></td><td><p><code>am-flights.us-e2.stgx.cloudhub.io</code></p></td></tr><tr><td><p><code>class</code></p></td><td><p>Java class that generates the log.</p></td><td><p><code>[am-flights].am-flights-api-httpListenerConfig.worker.34</code></p></td></tr><tr><td><p><code>environment</code></p></td><td><p>CloudHub environment name.</p></td><td><p><code>myEnv</code></p></td></tr><tr><td><p><code>environment type</code></p></td><td><p>CloudHub environment type</p></td><td><p><code>Sandbox</code>, <code>Design</code>, <code>Production</code></p></td></tr><tr><td><p><code>log level</code></p></td><td><p>ALL, DEBUG, ERROR, FATAL, INFO, OFF, TRACE, WARN</p></td><td><p><code>INFO</code></p></td></tr><tr><td><p><code>logger</code></p></td><td><p>Log4J Logger class</p></td><td><p><code>HttpListenerRegistry</code></p></td></tr><tr><td><p><code>message</code></p></td><td><p>Log4J message</p></td><td><p><code>No listener found for request: (POST)/zaraza</code></p></td></tr><tr><td><p><code>timestamp</code></p></td><td><p>Timestamp on the log. Accepted formats: <code>MMM dd yyyy, HH:mm:ss.SSS</code></p></td><td><div><div><p>Supported time settings are <code>s</code> for seconds, <code>m</code> for minutes, <code>h</code> for hours, <code>d</code> for days, <code>w</code> for weeks, <code>M</code> for months, <code>y</code> for years, with <code>now</code> for relative times, for example:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>Last 5 minutes: <code>now-5m</code></p></li><li><p>The day so far: <code>now/d</code></p></li><li><p>This week: <code>now/w</code></p></li><li><p>Week to date: <code>now/w</code></p></li><li><p>Previous Month: <code>now-1M/M</code></p></li></ul></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><code>worker</code></p></td><td><p>CloudHub worker ID.</p></td><td><p><code>0</code>, <code>1</code></p></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Operators

You can use these operators when creating a search filter. For example `log-level IS`

<table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><tbody><tr><td><p><code>is</code></p></td><td><p>Available to all filters.</p></td></tr><tr><td><p><code>is not</code></p></td><td><p>Available to all filters.</p></td></tr><tr><td><p><code>is between</code></p></td><td><p>Available to <code>timestamp</code>.</p></td></tr><tr><td><p><code>is not between</code></p></td><td><p>Available to <code>timestamp</code>.</p></td></tr><tr><td><p><code>exists</code></p></td><td><p>Available to all filters.</p></td></tr><tr><td><p><code>does not exist</code></p></td><td><p>Available to all filters.</p></td></tr></tbody></table>

## Filtering Based on the Entire Log Field Value

You can use the funnel-shaped icons on any log in your results to create and remove a filter for the
entire log field value.

![Funnel icon used to create a log message filter](_images/log-filter-create-funnel.png)

The filter you create is visible as a filter tag in the Filters area at the top of the page, for
example:

![Log message filter tag](_images/log-filter-tag.png)

You can edit and use that filter like any other filter. See [Search Filters](#filters_predefined).

## Share Logs

You can share the URL to your logs.

1.  From Logs, click **Share**.
2.  Select **Share** to load the URL of your log page to a **Share Logs Search Link** field.
3.  Click **Copy**.

## Save and View Saved Log Searches

Save and view log searches.

1.  Click **Save Search**.
2.  Select **View saved searches**.
3.  Click **View Saved Searches** again to return to the main Logs page.

## Clear a Search

You can clear the current search from the same menu shown in
[Save and View Saved Log Searches](#save_view_log_searches):

1.  Click **Save Search**.
2.  Select **Clear Current Search**.

### See also

[Search Query Syntax](log-search-query-syntax)
---

# Using Logs in Anypoint Monitoring

> For the full documentation index, see: https://docs.mulesoft.com/llms.txt

Use Anypoint Monitoring to retrieve logs on your deployed applications:

- [US and EU](logs-us-eu) Anypoint Platform clouds
  - **Log Search** for logs across your deployed applications
  - **Log Points** for real-time log generation
  - **Raw Data** for raw-data downloads

- [Japan and Canada](logs-jp-ca) Anypoint Platform clouds
  - **Logs** for logs across your deployed applications
---

# Setting Up Monitoring for Runtime Fabric Deployments

> For the full documentation index, see: https://docs.mulesoft.com/llms.txt

Monitoring for applications deployed to Runtime Fabric is enabled by default.

## Supported Versions of Mule Runtime Engine

Run your Mule app on a supported Mule runtime version. For supported versions, see
[Mule Runtime Release Notes](../release-notes/mule-runtime/mule-esb).

## Runtime Fabric Setup

Follow the instructions for
[Setting Up Runtime Fabric](../runtime-fabric/latest/setting-up-runtime-fabric). After you install
Runtime Fabric, applications that are running and deployed to Runtime Fabric appear in Monitoring.

### See Also

[Monitoring Applications Deployed to Runtime Fabric](../runtime-fabric/latest/manage-monitor-applications).
---

# Getting Performance Metrics from Connectors

> For the full documentation index, see: https://docs.mulesoft.com/llms.txt

[![](../_/img/icons/info.svg)](/monitoring/#billing)

View performance metrics for supported connectors in your Mule apps in Anypoint Monitoring
dashboards. Built-in dashboards and Anypoint Insights support these connector metrics.

## Supported Mule Versions

To monitor connectors, you must use a
[supported Mule version](../release-notes/mule-runtime/mule-esb) for your apps:

- Hybrid deployments
- CloudHub and CloudHub 2.0 deployments

> [!NOTE] If your app uses Java 17, you must specify the version during deployment. For a list of
> compatible connectors, see
> [Java 17 Compatible Anypoint Connectors](https://help.salesforce.com/s/articleView?language=en_US&id=000782248&type=1).

## Connector Metrics

The type of connector determines the charts that are displayed. Unless otherwise specified, the
following metrics are for outbound requests only.

<table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Connector</th><th>Charts</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Anypoint MQ (Inbound)</strong></p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p>Requests: Average number of Anypoint MQ connector inbound requests, grouped by operation and automatic intervals</p></li><li><p>Response Time: Average response time of Anypoint MQ connector inbound requests, grouped by operation and automatic intervals</p></li><li><p>Failures: Average number of Anypoint MQ connector inbound request failures, grouped by operation and automatic intervals</p></li></ul></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Anypoint MQ (Outbound)</strong></p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p>Requests: Average number of Anypoint MQ connector outbound requests, grouped by operation and automatic intervals</p></li><li><p>Response Time: Average response time of Anypoint MQ connector outbound requests, grouped by operation and automatic intervals</p></li><li><p>Failures: Average number of Anypoint MQ connector outbound request failures, grouped by operation and automatic intervals</p></li></ul></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Database</strong></p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p>Requests: Average number of Database connector requests, grouped by automatic intervals</p></li><li><p>Response Time: Average response time of Database connector requests, grouped by automatic intervals</p></li><li><p>Failures: Average number of Database connector request failures, grouped by automatic intervals</p></li></ul></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>FTP</strong></p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p>Requests: Average number of FTP connector requests, grouped by automatic intervals</p></li><li><p>Response Time: Average response time of FTP connector requests, grouped by automatic intervals</p></li><li><p>Failures: Average number of FTP connector request failures, grouped by automatic intervals</p></li></ul></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>HTTP</strong></p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p>Requests: Average number of HTTP connector requests, grouped by automatic intervals</p></li><li><p>Response Time: Average response time of HTTP connector requests, grouped by automatic intervals</p></li><li><p>Failures: Average number of HTTP connector request failures, grouped by automatic intervals</p></li></ul></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Object Store</strong></p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p>Requests: Average number of Object Store connector requests, grouped by operation and automatic intervals</p></li><li><p>Response Time: Average response time of Object Store connector requests, grouped by operation and automatic intervals</p></li><li><p>Failures: Average number of Object Store connector request failures, grouped by operation and automatic intervals</p></li></ul></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Salesforce</strong></p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p>Requests: Average number of Salesforce connector requests, grouped by automatic intervals</p></li><li><p>Response Time: Average response time of Salesforce connector requests, grouped by automatic intervals</p></li><li><p>Failures: Average number of Salesforce connector request failures, grouped by automatic intervals</p></li></ul></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>SFTP</strong></p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p>Requests: Average number of SFTP connector requests, grouped by automatic intervals</p></li><li><p>Response Time: Average response time of SFTP connector requests, grouped by automatic intervals</p></li><li><p>Failures: Average number of SFTP connector request failures, grouped by automatic intervals</p></li></ul></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>SMTP</strong></p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p>Requests: Average number of SMTP connector requests, grouped by automatic intervals</p></li><li><p>Response Time: Average response time of SMTP connector requests, grouped by automatic intervals</p></li><li><p>Failures: Average number of SMTP connector request failures, grouped by automatic intervals</p></li></ul></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Web Services</strong></p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p>Requests: Average number of Web Services connector requests, grouped by automatic intervals</p></li><li><p>Response Time: Average response time of Web Services connector requests, grouped by automatic intervals</p></li><li><p>Failures: Average number of Web Services connector request failures, grouped by automatic intervals</p></li></ul></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Other</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>The connector type "Other" is any connector not specified in this table.</p></div><div><ul><li><p>Requests: Average number of requests of connectors of type Other, grouped by automatic intervals</p></li><li><p>Response Time: Average response time of requests of connectors of type Other, grouped by automatic intervals</p></li><li><p>Failures: Average number of request failures of other types of connectors, grouped by automatic intervals</p></li></ul></div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

## Access Connector Monitoring

1.  Log in to Anypoint Platform with a user account that has
    [permission to access Anypoint Monitoring content](am-permissions).
2.  Navigate to Anypoint Monitoring:

    Click **Monitoring** from the Anypoint Platform home page or from the **Runtimes** menu in the
    header.

3.  Open **Insights** or **Built-in dashboards**.
4.  Navigate to a Mule app that uses a supported connector.
5.  In the detail page for the app, click **Connectors** to view connector metrics.

    If you donât see the **Connectors** tab, verify that you have the Anypoint Integration Advanced
    package or a Titanium subscription to Anypoint Platform and that the app you selected is running
    on a supported version of Mule.
---
