# Managing CloudHub and Hybrid Settings

> For the full documentation index, see: https://docs.mulesoft.com/llms.txt

On the **Settings** page in Anypoint Monitoring, you can:

- Find the instructions to install Anypoint Monitoring on an on-premises server to monitor Mule
  applications running on that server.
- Enable (or disable) monitoring for applications running on Mule.

## Access the Settings Page

To access the **Settings** page, you must have the Anypoint Monitoring User and the Runtime
Manager - Read Applications permissions. See
[Granting Permissions and Roles to Users](../access-management/users#granting-permissions-and-roles-to-users).

1.  Log in to Anypoint Platform with a user account that has
    [permission to access Anypoint Monitoring content](am-permissions).
2.  Navigate to Anypoint Monitoring:

    Click **Monitoring** from the Anypoint Platform home page or from the **Runtimes** menu in the
    header.

3.  In the sidebar, click **Settings**.
4.  To monitor applications deployed to a local server, select **Hybrid**, and follow the procedure
    to install Anypoint Monitoring agent from the latest version of the agentâs installer in
    `am.zip`.
5.  To enable (or disable) monitoring for apps deployed to CloudHub, select **CloudHub**.

> [!NOTE] Anypoint Monitoring is enabled by default for all CloudHub 2.0 applications.

## See Also

- [Install Anypoint Monitoring On-Premises](am-installing)
- [Configure Anypoint Monitoring for Mule Apps](configure-monitoring-cloudhub)
---

# Understanding Performance Impact, Limits, and Data Retention

> For the full documentation index, see: https://docs.mulesoft.com/llms.txt

Anypoint Monitoring processes and retains massive amounts of data with a low performance impact.
Titanium subscribers receive an enhanced data retention package and more storage than Gold and
Platinum subscribers. For better performance and readability, your log data is truncated into short
lines.

You can also improve your Anypoint Monitoring experience by adjusting your screen resolution.

For core-based subscription tier, see
[Anypoint Monitoring Features for Legacy Subscription-Based Plans](./#plans-legacy).

## Mule Monitoring Agent Performance Impact

Based on typical use, the expected CPU utilization from the Mule monitoring agent is less than 5%.
For more information, refer to
[Configure and Enable Anypoint Monitoring for Mule Apps (CloudHub)](configure-monitoring-cloudhub#performance-impact).

Anypoint Monitoring asynchronously collects data at a resolution of one minute, which does not block
Mule runtime data processing.

## Data Retention Limits

Limits vary by [subscription tier](./#billing):

- [Anypoint Integration Starter Package Limits](#starter)
- [Anypoint Integration Advanced Package Limits](#advanced)
- [Gold and Platinum Subscription Limits](#gold-platinum)
- [Titanium Subscription Limits](#titanium)

### Anypoint Integration Starter Package Limits

Organizations that have the Anypoint Integration Starter package receive modest data retention and
feature limits:

Logs

Log limits are documented in [Runtime Manager](../cloudhub/viewing-log-data#log-persistence).

Application metrics

30 days.

Enhanced API metrics

30 days. Data in the **Client Applications** tab is limited to 1 hour.

### Anypoint Integration Advanced Package Limits

Organizations that have the Anypoint Integration Advanced package receive more robust data retention
and feature limits:

Logs

In raw format, your logs can occupy as much space as you receive with your pricing package. Note
that the storage space you receive with your pricing package is used to store logs and metrics data.
Because metrics do not take up much storage capacity, you can use the majority of your storage for
raw log data.

Searchable logs

You receive additional storage that is 10% of the size of purchased storage for indexes for
searchable logs.

Anypoint Monitoring creates indexes on raw logs to enable you to efficiently and quickly perform log
search queries. Anypoint Monitoring indexes the most recent logs for searching, up to 10% of the
total purchased storage size. You can retrieve older logs with the
[Anypoint Monitoring Archive API](https://anypoint.mulesoft.com/exchange/portals/anypoint-platform/f1e97bc6-315a-4490-82a7-23abe036327a.anypoint-platform/anypoint-monitoring-archive-api/minor/1.0/pages/Metric%20Data/).

Total storage capacity is shared globally across all apps and is limited by the number of messages
and flows that your organization purchases and the maximum searchable storage allocation limit of
6TB.

- For every flow purchased, this subscription tier receives:
  - 5GB of archival storage
  - 0.5GB of searchable storage (10% of archival storage)

- For every 1M messages purchased, this subscription tier receives:
  - 2.5GB of archival storage
  - 0.25GB of searchable storage (10% of archival storage)

Application metrics

365 days for built-in dashboards

For Anypoint Insights, see [Monitoring Metrics from Anypoint Insights](anypoint-insights).

Enhanced API metrics

30 days. These limits also apply:

- Data in the **Client Applications** tab is limited to 1 hour.
- Data from **api_path_metric** and **api_client_ip_metric** tables in advanced mode of custom
  dashboards is limited to 1 hour.

  Metrics data can persist beyond the data retention period. The data is removed when the automated
  cleanup cycle takes place.

Custom metrics

365 days in production environments

Custom dashboard alerts

10 per app or API instance.

Basic alerts for servers and Mule apps

10 per server or app.

Basic alerts for APIs

10 per server or app.

Telemetry Exporter Connections

10 per organization.

### Gold and Platinum Subscription Limits

[Gold and Platinum tier](./#plans-legacy) subscribers to Anypoint Platform receive modest data
retention and feature limits:

Logs

Log limits are documented in [Runtime Manager](../cloudhub/viewing-log-data#log-persistence).

Application metrics

30 days.

Enhanced API metrics

30 days. These limits also apply:

- Data in the **Client Applications** tab is limited to 1 hour.
- Data from **api_path_metric** and **api_client_ip_metric** tables in advanced mode of custom
  dashboards is limited to 1 hour.

  Metrics data can persist beyond the data retention period. The data is removed when the automated
  cleanup cycle takes place.

Basic alerts for servers and Mule apps

50 basic alerts.

Basic alerts for APIs

Six basic alerts per API instance.

### Titanium Subscription Limits

[Titanium tier](./#plans-legacy) subscribers to Anypoint Platform receive more robust data retention
and feature limits:

Logs

In raw format, your logs can occupy as much space as you receive with your Titanium subscription.
Note that the storage space you receive with your Titanium subscription is used to store logs and
metrics data. Because metrics do not take up much storage capacity, you can use the majority of your
storage for raw log data. For more information about logs or to request deletion of your logs, see
[Anypoint Monitoring Log FAQ](https://help.salesforce.com/s/articleView?id=001118219&type=1).

Searchable logs

You receive additional storage that is 10% of the size of purchased storage for indexes for
searchable logs.

Anypoint Monitoring creates indexes on raw logs to enable you to efficiently and quickly perform log
search queries. Anypoint Monitoring indexes the most recent logs for searching, up to 10% of the
total purchased storage size. You can download logs to access older logs.

Total storage capacity is shared globally across all apps and is limited by the number of messages
and flows that your organization purchases and the maximum searchable storage allocation limit of
6TB.

- For each production vCore, this subscription tier receives:
  - 200GB of archival storage
  - 20GB of searchable storage (10% of archival storage)

- For each pre-production vCore, this subscription tier receives:
  - 50GB of archival storage
  - 5GB of searchable storage (10% of archival storage)

Application metrics

365 days for built-in dashboards

For Anypoint Insights, see [Monitoring Metrics from Anypoint Insights](anypoint-insights).

Enhanced API metrics

30 days. These limits also apply:

- Data in the **Client Applications** tab is limited to 1 hour.
- Data from **api_path_metric** and **api_client_ip_metric** tables in advanced mode of custom
  dashboards is limited to 1 hour.

  Metrics data can persist beyond the data retention period. The data is removed when the automated
  cleanup cycle takes place.

Custom metrics

365 days in production environments

Custom dashboard alerts

20 per parent organization.

Basic alerts for servers and Mule apps

50 times the number of production vCores in your organization plus 100. For example, an organization
that has 2 production vCores and a Titanium subscription has a limit of 200 basic alerts. Note that
you can also use your alert allowance for sandbox deployments, but this limit subject to the overall
[production vCore limit](../access-management/business-groups#redistribute-resources).

Basic alerts for APIs

Ten basic alerts per API instance.

Telemetry Exporter Connections

10 per organization.

## Logging Data Management

When you manage logs, the maximum length of one line of log data is 8 KB (including date and thread
metadata). Single lines that are longer than 8 KB are truncated at the 8 KB mark.

To log longer segments of information, you must split them up into chunks that are individually
smaller than 8 KB.

## Screen Resolution

Use a minimum screen resolution of 1024 by 768 pixels.

## Grace Period for Metrics Data Retention

If your organization downgrades to a lower subscription tier (usage-based or core-based) or cancels
your subscription, your metrics data is removed according to this schedule:

- Downgrades: Approximately 14 days after a downgrade to a lower tier, an additional 21-day grace
  period takes place before your data is removed.
- Cancellations: Approximately 90 days after a cancellation, an additional 21-day grace period takes
  place before your data is removed.

To retrieve metrics data, you can use the
[Anypoint Monitoring Archive API](https://anypoint.mulesoft.com/exchange/portals/anypoint-platform/f1e97bc6-315a-4490-82a7-23abe036327a.anypoint-platform/anypoint-monitoring-archive-api/minor/1.0/pages/Metric%20Data/).
---

# Getting Started

> For the full documentation index, see: https://docs.mulesoft.com/llms.txt

Anypoint Monitoring provides data on Mule apps, APIs, and instances of agents, brokers, and MCP
servers.

> [!NOTE] Functional Monitoring is covered in
> [API Functional Monitoring](../api-functional-monitoring/).

## Supported Versions of Mule Runtime Engine

Run your Mule app on a supported Mule runtime version. For supported versions, see
[Mule Runtime Release Notes](../release-notes/mule-runtime/mule-esb).

## Use Anypoint Monitoring

1.  Log in to Anypoint Platform with a user account that has
    [permission to access Anypoint Monitoring content](am-permissions).
2.  Navigate to Anypoint Monitoring:

    Click **Monitoring** from the Anypoint Platform home page or from the **Runtimes** menu in the
    header.

    If you do not find **Monitoring** or are unable to use Anypoint Monitoring, check your
    permissions ([Required Permission](#required_permission)).

3.  From the sidebar, you can find [Anypoint Monitoring features](./#features).

## Configurations

Custom dashboards and alerts require configuration before you can use them. For guidance, see:

- [Configuring Custom Dashboards](dashboard-custom-config)
- [Configuring Alerts](alerts)

## Required Permission

[Monitoring permissions](../access-management/permissions-by-product#monitoring) lists user and
administrator permissions.

Typically, administrators set permissions through Access Management. See
[Granting Permissions and Roles to Users](../access-management/users#granting-permissions-and-roles-to-users).

## Enable Anypoint Monitoring on CloudHub

For Anypoint Monitoring to start monitoring your Mule apps, you must deploy them to the appropriate
version of Mule runtime engine. See
[Configure Anypoint Monitoring for Mule Apps (CloudHub)](configure-monitoring-cloudhub).

## Enable Anypoint Monitoring On-Premises

You can [install Anypoint Monitoring](am-installing) on an on-premises server to monitor
applications that are running on that server and managed via Runtime Manager (hybrid apps).

## Access Key Metrics through the Anypoint Monitoring Agent

Access to [Key Metrics in API Manager](../api-manager/latest/analytics-chart) relies on the Anypoint
Monitoring agent. The agent is available by default for some Mule app deployment models. Other
models require enablement of Anypoint Monitoring or installation of the agent.

<table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Mule App Deployment Model</th><th>Action</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>CloudHub 1.0</p></td><td><p>Enable Anypoint Monitoring for your Mule Apps. See <a href="configure-monitoring-cloudhub">Setting Up Monitoring for CloudHub Deployments</a>.</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>CloudHub 2.0</p></td><td><p>No action is required. Key Metrics and Anypoint Monitoring are available by default.</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Runtime Fabric</p></td><td><p>No action is required. Key Metrics and Anypoint Monitoring are available by default. See also, <a href="monitor-applications-on-rtf">Setting Up Monitoring for Runtime Fabric Deployments</a> and <a href="monitor-applications-on-rtf#supported-versions-of-mule-runtime-engine">Supported Versions of Mule Runtime Engine</a>.</p><p><a id="hybrid"></a></p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Hybrid Deployments</p></td><td><p>Install the Anypoint Monitoring agent. See <a href="am-installing">Setting Up Monitoring for Hybrid Deployments</a>. If you proxy your monitoring data as it leaves the data center, you must configure the Anypoint Monitoring agent to communicate through the proxy. See <a href="am-installing#proxy-settings">Proxy Settings</a>.</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Standalone Deployments On-Prem</p></td><td><p>Key Metrics are not supported because standalone deployments are not managed in Anypoint Platform. If you require Key Metrics, convert your standalone deployment to a hybrid deployment (see <a href="../runtime-manager/servers-create">Add Servers to Runtime Manager</a>), and install the Anypoint Monitoring agent (see Hybrid Deployments).</p></td></tr></tbody></table>

## See Also

- [Anypoint Monitoring Settings](monitoring-settings-page)
- [Configure Anypoint Monitoring for Mule Apps](configure-monitoring-cloudhub)
- [Install Anypoint Monitoring On-Premises](am-installing)
- [Create a Monitor](../api-functional-monitoring/afm-create-monitor)
---

# Downloading Raw Data (US and EU)

> For the full documentation index, see: https://docs.mulesoft.com/llms.txt

[![](../_/img/icons/info.svg)](/monitoring/#billing)

On [US](https://anypoint.mulesoft.com/monitoring) and
[EU](https://eu1.anypoint.mulesoft.com/monitoring) Clouds, you can use Anypoint Monitoring to
download archived log data (raw data) collected on Mule apps that are running in hybrid, CloudHub,
or Runtime Fabric deployments. This data is helpful for security, compliance, or other purposes
where you need to examine or collect your warehoused data.

> [!NOTE] Log data is archived regularly.

To download raw data:

1.  Select the **Environment** and **Resource**.

    The resource is a Mule app that runs in CloudHub, hybrid, Runtime Fabric deployments.

2.  Provide the date for the data you want to download, for example: `08/31/2018`.
3.  Locate and download log data that you want to examine.

If data is not available for a particular date, it might be that the app was not running that day,
your company did not have a subscription to the logging or archiving service, or the app did not
exist at that time.

## See Also

[Logs in Anypoint Monitoring](logs)
---

# Viewing Reports in Anypoint Monitoring

> For the full documentation index, see: https://docs.mulesoft.com/llms.txt

[![](../_/img/icons/info.svg)](/monitoring/#billing)

View reports on Mule app performance and behavior across all apps in an environment. Reports help
you maintain the expected level of service for your apps.

![List of reports on the Reports page](_images/reports-hf.png)

<table><tbody><tr><td><i data-value="1"></i><b>1</b></td><td>Selects an environment for Mule apps, such as a production or sandbox environment.<div><p>In US and EU clouds only, the classic version of Anypoint Monitoring provides the environment selector on a separate page.</p></div></td></tr><tr><td><i data-value="2"></i><b>2</b></td><td>Displays reports:<div><ul><li><p>All deployment types provide <strong>Requests</strong>, <strong>Performance</strong>, and <strong>Failures</strong> reports.</p></li><li><p>CloudHub deployments provide <strong>CPU Utilization</strong> and <strong>Memory Utilization</strong> reports.</p></li></ul></div></td></tr><tr><td><i data-value="3"></i><b>3</b></td><td>Lists apps you can click to get more details about the app:<div><ul><li><p>Review the app target, request volume, and number of successful requests in the selected time range.</p><div><p>You can also sort columns by clicking a column heading.</p></div></li><li><p>Clicking an app opens the details page in <strong>Insights</strong>.</p><div><div data-type="NOTE">In US and EU clouds only, clicking an app in <strong>Reports</strong> from the classic version of Anypoint Monitoring opens to the app details page in <strong>Built-in Dashboards</strong>.</div></div></li></ul></div></td></tr><tr><td><i data-value="4"></i><b>4</b></td><td>Sets a lookback period for the reports (24 hours and 7, 15, 30, and 90 days)</td></tr><tr><td><i data-value="5"></i><b>5</b></td><td>Manually refreshes report data</td></tr><tr><td><i data-value="6"></i><b>6</b></td><td>Downloads report data from the selected tab to CSV format</td></tr></tbody></table>

## Access Reports

1.  Log in to Anypoint Platform with a user account that has
    [permission to access Anypoint Monitoring content](am-permissions).
2.  Navigate to Anypoint Monitoring:

    Click **Monitoring** from the Anypoint Platform home page or from the **Runtimes** menu in the
    header.

3.  In the sidebar, click **Reports**.
4.  From **Environment**, select the environment where the apps you want to view are deployed.
5.  Select the lookback period to view reports for that time range.
6.  Click a report type:
    - [Requests](#requests)
    - [Performance](#performance)
    - [Failures](#failures)
    - [CPU Utilization](#cpu)
    - [Memory Utilization](#memory)

### Requests

Click the **Requests** tab to view information about app requests, including:

- **Request Volume**: The total number of inbound requests to the app in the specified time range.
- **Successful Requests**: The total number of successful inbound requests to the app within the
  specified time range.

### Performance

Click the **Performance** tab to view information about app performance metrics, including:

- **Request Volume**: The total number of inbound requests to the app in the specified time range.
- **Response Time**: The average response time (in milliseconds) for inbound requests to the app.

### Failures

Click the **Failures** tab to view information about app request failures, including:

- **Request Volume**: The total number of inbound requests to the app in the specified time range.
- **Successful Requests**: The number of successful inbound requests to the app in the specified
  time range.
- **Failed Requests**: The total number of failed inbound requests to the app in the specified time
  range. An inbound request failure occurs when a Mule flow in your deployed Mule app throws an
  exception. Inbound failures differ from outbound failures, which indicate exceptions from outbound
  connector operations, such as from an HTTP Request operation in the Anypoint Connector for HTTP
  (HTTP Connector). Failure counts apply only to exceptions from the app, so a 4xx or 5xx reply code
  to a caller isnât a failure unless the reply is due to an unmanaged exception.

### CPU Utilization

Click the **CPU Utilization** tab to view information about the average percentage of the CPU
utilized by the JVM.

> [!NOTE] This tab isnât available to apps in
> [hybrid standalone deployments](../hybrid-standalone/).

### Memory Utilization

Click the **Memory Utilization** tab to view information about the memory utilization of each app
running in the environment, including:

- **Memory Utilization**: The amount of the heap memory used by the JVM.
- **Total Memory**: The total size of the appâs heap memory.
- **Memory Pressure**: The percentage of heap memory in use, calculated as memory used divided by
  total memory.

> [!NOTE] This tab isnât available to apps in
> [hybrid standalone deployments](../hybrid-standalone/).

## Export a Report as a CSV

Export a report on all apps in the selected tab as a CSV file.

1.  Navigate to a report.

    For guidance, see [Access Reports](#access-reports).

2.  Click **Export CSV** to download the CSV file.
---

# Saving a Dashboard as a Custom Dashboard

> For the full documentation index, see: https://docs.mulesoft.com/llms.txt

[![](../_/img/icons/info.svg)](/monitoring/#billing)

To make creating a custom dashboard easier, you can save any of the built-in dashboards that are
included in the Anypoint Monitoring tabs as a custom dashboard that you can then customize to suit
your needs. This allows you to take advantage of the flexibility and features offered in custom
dashboards, such as troubleshooting, setting up alerts, or comparing multiple resources within one
dashboard.

Follow these steps to save a built-in dashboard as a custom dashboard.

1.  Log in to Anypoint Platform with a user account that has
    [permission to access Anypoint Monitoring content](am-permissions).
2.  Navigate to Anypoint Monitoring:

    Click **Monitoring** from the Anypoint Platform home page or from the **Runtimes** menu in the
    header.

3.  In the sidebar, click **Built-in dashboards**.
4.  Click the tab for the built-in dashboard to save, for example, **Performance**.
5.  Click the **Save as** icon.
6.  In the **Save as** dialog, enter a **Name** for your custom dashboard, and click **Save**.

    > [!NOTE] The name for your new custom dashboard must be unique.

A green confirmation banner appears in the top of the screen. Click the **View in custom
dashboards** link in the green confirmation banner to see the new custom dashboard, with the name
you entered, in the list of custom dashboards.  
The banner disappears after a moment. If you didnât click the link in the banner, you can click
**Custom dashboards** in the menu on the left to go to the list of custom dashboards.  
You can now configure your custom dashboard.

## See Also

[Configuring Custom Dashboards and Charts](dashboard-custom-config)
---

# Exporting Telemetry Data to Third-Party Monitoring Systems

> For the full documentation index, see: https://docs.mulesoft.com/llms.txt

[![](../_/img/icons/info.svg)](/monitoring/#billing)

Export Mule app trace data and [audit logs](../access-management/audit-logging) to third-party
observability platforms like Azure Monitor, Splunk HEC, and OpenTelemetry-compliant tools.

To use the Telemetry Exporter, you must:

- Create a connection by providing a third-party observability platform credential that authorizes
  Anypoint Platform to share data with the observability platform. See also,
  [Supported Third-Party Monitoring Products](#third-party-support).
- Create a configuration that specifies the type of data Anypoint Platform exports and which
  business group and environment allows content to be exported to the connections that you
  configure.

You can also edit and delete connections and configurations. Telemetry Exporter checks for changes
to connections and configurations on an hourly basis. When you create or modify a connection or
configuration, there might be a delay in applying your new settings.

When exported, audit logs have a unique ID in the `mulesoft.audit.id` attribute. In usual
operations, each log is delivered only once, but under certain circumstances, some logs are
delivered more than once. Duplicate log entries always share the unique `mulesoft.audit.id`
attribute, so this attribute can be used to safely block or remove duplicate logs from the target
system.

## Supported Third-Party Monitoring Products

The Telemetry Exporter enables you to create connections to most third-party observability and
analytics platforms that:

- Have OpenTelemetry-supported endpoints. For example, the endpoint URL for New Relic is
  `[https://otlp.nr-data.net:4318](https://otlp.nr-data.net:4318)`. Your team must set up endpoints
  for the monitoring platform you use.
- Allow you to create an instrumentation key or access token.

Azure Monitor and Splunk HEC are also compatible.

## Before You Begin

Before you configure the Telemetry Exporter, ensure that you have:

- Permissions that you require for your uses of Telemetry Exporter (see
  [Managing Permissions for Anypoint Monitoring](am-permissions))
  - **Telemetry Exporter Administrator** permission for creating and modifying connections
  - **Telemetry Exporter Configurations Manager** permission for creating and modifying
    configurations

- Access information for your third-party observability platform
- The ability to create an instrumentation key or access token for your third-party observability
  platform.
- Enabled trace data collection for your Mule apps:
  - For CloudHub 2.0 deployments, see
    [shared](../cloudhub-2/ch2-deploy-shared-space#enable-trace-data-collection) and
    [private spaces](../cloudhub-2/ch2-deploy-private-space#enable-trace-data-collection).
  - For Runtime Fabric deployments, see
    [Runtime Fabric](../runtime-fabric/latest/use-anypoint-monitoring#enable-trace-data).

- An endpoint for telemetry data that is accessible from across the internet (CIDR `0.0.0.0/0`) if
  your observability provider or your organizationâs endpoint requires an IP allowlist or firewall
  rule

  Consult your organizationâs security guidelines when configuring open access rules for this
  endpoint. Telemetry Exporter generates its Internet Protocol (IP) address dynamically, which
  prevents allowlisting specific IPs, URLs, domains.

## Access Telemetry Exporter

1.  Log in to Anypoint Platform using an account that has the Telemetry Exporter Administrator or
    Telemetry Exporter Configurations Manager permission.
2.  Navigate to Anypoint Monitoring:

    Click **Monitoring** from the Anypoint Platform home page or from the **Runtimes** menu in the
    header.

3.  Click **Telemetry Exporter**.

To use create, view, edit, and delete connections and configurations for telemetry data exports, see
[Manage a Connection](#manage-connections) and [Manage Configurations](#manage-configurations).

## Manage a Connection

Anypoint Platform needs permission to send data to third-party observability platforms. Create a
connection with a third-party platform, such as New Relic or Splunk, to authorize data transfers.
You can configure up to 10 connections.

Telemetry Exporter checks for changes to connections and configurations on an hourly basis. When you
create or modify a connection or configuration, there might be a delay in applying your new
settings.

### Create a Connection

1.  Log in to Anypoint Platform using an account that has the Telemetry Exporter Administrator
    permission.
2.  Navigate to Anypoint Monitoring:

    Click **Monitoring** from the Anypoint Platform home page or from the **Runtimes** menu in the
    header.

3.  Click **Telemetry Exporter**.
4.  Click the **Connections** tab.
5.  Click **New Connection**.
6.  On the **Connection Details** page, enter the following information:
    - **Connection Name**: Name for your connection, using only alphanumeric characters and hyphens.
    - **Destination Type**: Third-party observability platform to which you want to transfer data.
    - **Endpoint URL**: Endpoint that enables external connections to your third-party observability
      platform (see [Supported Third-Party Monitoring Products](#third-party-support)). Some
      platforms, such as DataDog, require an OpenTelemetry collector endpoint that is enabled to
      translate the OpenTelemetry standard to DataDog formats.

7.  Click **Next**.
8.  On the **Authentication** page, enter the information needed to authenticate your connection to
    the observability platform. Some platforms require only access tokens, while others also require
    a header key.
9.  Click **Test Connection** to ensure that Anypoint Platform can connect to your third-party
    observability platform.
10. Click **Save Connection**.

    The new connection appears in the **Connections** tab on the **Telemetry Exporter** page.

### Edit a Connection

1.  Log in to Anypoint Platform using an account that has the Telemetry Exporter Administrator
    permission.
2.  Navigate to Anypoint Monitoring:

    Click **Monitoring** from the Anypoint Platform home page or from the **Runtimes** menu in the
    header.

3.  Click **Telemetry Exporter**.
4.  Click the **Connections** tab.
5.  Next to the connection you want to edit, click **â¦â** and select **Edit Connection**.
6.  On the **Connection Details** page, edit each field you want to change and click **Next**.
7.  On the **Authentication** page, edit each field you want to change and click **Test
    Connection**.
8.  If the connection is successful, click **Save Connection**.

### Delete a Connection

1.  Log in to Anypoint Platform using an account that has the Telemetry Exporter Administrator
    permission.
2.  Navigate to Anypoint Monitoring:

    Click **Monitoring** from the Anypoint Platform home page or from the **Runtimes** menu in the
    header.

3.  Click **Telemetry Exporter**.
4.  Next to the connection you want to delete, click **â¦â** and select **Delete Connection**.
5.  A warning appears:
    - If the connection is not in use, the warning asks whether to delete the connection. Click
      **Delete Connection**.
    - If the connection is in use, the warning states that you must delete or redirect connections
      for any configurations that use the connection. Click **Dismiss**, and then
      [edit your configurations](#edit-configuration) to use other existing connections before
      deleting the current connection.

## Manage Configurations

After you create a connection to your third-party observability platform, you must create a
configuration to specify the scope of the data to send to the platform. When you create a new
configuration, specify a data type, business group, environment type (traces only), and destination.
You can export traces and audit logs from all of your business groups or a specific business group.

Telemetry Exporter checks for changes to connections and configurations on an hourly basis. When you
create or modify a connection or configuration, there might be a delay in applying your new
settings.

### Create a Configuration

1.  Log in to Anypoint Platform using an account that has the Telemetry Exporter Configurations
    Manager permission.
2.  Navigate to Anypoint Monitoring:

    Click **Monitoring** from the Anypoint Platform home page or from the **Runtimes** menu in the
    header.

3.  Click **Telemetry Exporter**.
4.  Click **New Configuration**.
5.  On the **General Information** page, enter the following information:
    - **Configuration Name**: Name for your configuration that uses only alphanumeric characters.
    - **Connection**: Existing connection that you configured.
    - **Data Type**: Audit logs or trace data

6.  Click **Next**.
7.  In the **Select Business Group** section, select one of the following:
    - **All business groups**: Audit logs are sourced from all business groups in the organization.
    - **Select a business group**: Data of the type you specified is sourced only from the selected
      business group.

8.  Click **Next**.
9.  If you selected trace data as the data type, select the environment type:
    - Production
    - Sandbox
    - Design

10. Click **Save Configuration**.

### Edit Configurations

When you edit configurations, you can change the configuration name and connection. If you want to
modify the data type business group or environment type, you must create a new configuration.

1.  Log in to Anypoint Platform using an account that has the Telemetry Exporter Configurations
    Manager permission.
2.  Navigate to Anypoint Monitoring:

    Click **Monitoring** from the Anypoint Platform home page or from the **Runtimes** menu in the
    header.

3.  Click **Telemetry Exporter**.
4.  Next to the configuration you want to edit, click **â¦â** and select **Edit Configuration**.
5.  On the **General Information** page, edit each field you want to change and click **Next**.
6.  On the **Business Group & Environment Type** page, **Next**.
7.  Click **Save Configuration**.

### Delete Configurations

1.  Log in to Anypoint Platform using an account that has the Telemetry Exporter Configurations
    Manager permission.
2.  Navigate to Anypoint Monitoring:

    Click **Monitoring** from the Anypoint Platform home page or from the **Runtimes** menu in the
    header.

3.  Click **Telemetry Exporter**.
4.  Next to the configuration you want to delete, click **â¦â** and select **Delete Connection**.

    A warning appears that confirms whether you want to delete the configuration.

5.  Click **Delete Configuration**.

## Errors

If you receive this error, ensure that your endpoint supports OpenTelemetry:

Error : validating connection request. Please check your request and try again

See [Supported Third-Party Monitoring Products](#third-party-support).

## Known Issues

Telemetry Exporter currently has the following data differences, limitations, and known issues:

- For failed requests, Telemetry Exporter retries are handled using the OpenTelemetry specifications
  for handling failures:
  - Permanent failures, such as bad requests, do not retry.
  - 5xx errors that can retry wait for a specific interval. Retries continue for 300 seconds before
    timing out.

- When you create configurations for the same connection, you cannot create a configuration that
  uses all business groups if there are configurations that use individual business groups.
  Conversely, you cannot create configurations for that connection if there is a configuration that
  uses all business groups.  
  For example, `Connection A` to Splunk has `Configuration A` and `Configuration B` that map to
  `Business Group A` and `Business Group B`. In this scenario, you are unable to use `Connection A`
  to create a configuration that exports data from all business groups.
- Because Azure Monitor Exporter does not support sending persistent queues, connections that use it
  might experience data loss. Data might be deleted from the sending queue when the Telemetry
  Exporter restarts hourly to apply updates.
- The audit logs that the Telemetry Exporter sends to third-party apps often have different field
  names from the audit logs that appear in Access Management or the Audit Query Log API.  
  For example, audit logs shown in the UI or retrieved by the Audit Log Query API describe a user
  action (such as `update` or `delete`) as `action`, whereas the OpenTelemetry attribute refers to
  it as `mulesoft.audit.action`.
- If the audit log entry metadata and payload field exceed 30KB when compressed, the payload is
  truncated before compression.
---

# Sending Notifications to External Systems

> For the full documentation index, see: https://docs.mulesoft.com/llms.txt

[![](../_/img/icons/info.svg)](/monitoring/#billing)

Anypoint Monitoring provides links to connectors on Anypoint Exchange that you can incorporate into
a Mule app to deliver notifications to external systems.

![Tools in Anypoint Monitoring](_images/tools.png)

Figure 1. Tools Available in Anypoint Monitoring

1.  Log in to Anypoint Platform with a user account that has
    [permission to access Anypoint Monitoring content](am-permissions).
2.  Navigate to Anypoint Monitoring:

    Click **Monitoring** from the Anypoint Platform home page or from the **Runtimes** menu in the
    header.

3.  In the sidebar, click **Tools**.
4.  Review the connectors.

    You can hover over a connector link to learn more before navigating to Anypoint Exchange.
---

# Examining Traces

> For the full documentation index, see: https://docs.mulesoft.com/llms.txt

[![](../_/img/icons/info.svg)](/monitoring/#billing)

On US and EU Cloud instances of Anypoint Platform, use Traces to track the flow of requests that
travel through service pipelines. Get data from the Open Telemetry (OTEL) spans that form your
traces. Troubleshoot requests with errors or slow response times, optimize your resource allocation,
and track calls end-to-end. Monitor the performance of a specific transaction as it travels through
services.

![Span list in Traces Overview](_images/traces.png)

<table><tbody><tr><td><i data-value="1"></i><b>1</b></td><td>Traces in Anypoint Monitoring</td></tr><tr><td><i data-value="2"></i><b>2</b></td><td><a href="#environments">Environment selector</a> for your deployments (such as Sandbox or Production)</td></tr><tr><td><i data-value="3"></i><b>3</b></td><td><a href="#filters">Filter</a> field for queries that narrow the list of logs to return</td></tr><tr><td><i data-value="4"></i><b>4</b></td><td>List of spans for the supported agents, brokers, MCP servers, and Mule apps</td></tr><tr><td><i data-value="5"></i><b>5</b></td><td>Lookback selector for the period of time to capture in the <strong>Span List</strong> (from the default of 15 minutes to the last seven days)</td></tr><tr><td><i data-value="6"></i><b>6</b></td><td>Auto-Refresh every 5, 15, or 30 minutes, every hour, or one time per day</td></tr><tr><td><i data-value="7"></i><b>7</b></td><td>Manual refresh button</td></tr></tbody></table>

In addition to reviewing traces in Anypoint Monitoring, you can use
[Telemetry Exporter](telemetry-exporter) to export traces to third-party observability and analytics
platforms.

## Traces Availability

Traces are available for specific:

- [Spans](#support-spans)
- [Hosts](#support-hosts)
- [Sampling Rates](#support-sampling)

### Supported Traces

Traces include spans for:

- Agentic assets: Agents, brokers, and MCP servers
- Flows: Mule flows for the selected task of the specific service
- Events: Mule runtime events or user actions for the selected service

### Hosts

Traces in Anypoint Monitoring displays spans from entities deployed to:

- CloudHub 2.0
- Anypoint Runtime Fabric (Limited Availabilty)

### Sampling Rate

Trace data sampling rate:

- 100%: Agents, brokers, and MCP servers
- 10%: Mule apps and APIs

## Before You Begin

For all types of traces, these prerequisites apply:

- The Anypoint Integration Advanced package or a Titanium subscription to Anypoint Platform
- [Permission to view](../access-management/permissions-by-product#monitoring) Mule apps, APIs, and
  agentic assets (agent, broker, and MCP server instances) in Anypoint Monitoring

For Mule app and API tracing, these prerequisites apply:

- For apps deployed to CloudHub 2.0:
  - [Enable trace data collection for your app in a shared space](../cloudhub-2/ch2-deploy-shared-space#enable-trace-data-collection)
  - [Enabled trace data collection for your app in a private space](../cloudhub-2/ch2-deploy-private-space#enable-trace-data-collection)

- For Runtime Fabric, enabled trace data collection on apps deployed to
  [Runtime Fabric](../runtime-fabric/latest/use-anypoint-monitoring#enable-trace-data)
- Mule runtime version 4.6 or later
- HTTP Connector version 1.8 or later

## Get Traces from Traces Overview

View a list of spans from across entities (agents, brokers, MCP servers, and Mule apps) in **Traces
Overview** in Anypoint Monitoring. Get high-level details on each root span in a trace. Use filters
to narrow the scope of spans in the **Spans List**. Get additional detail on each span in the
hierarchy of spans that form a trace (from root span to its descendants).

1.  Log in to Anypoint Platform with a user account that has
    [permission to access Anypoint Monitoring content](am-permissions).
2.  Navigate to Anypoint Monitoring:

    Click **Monitoring** from the Anypoint Platform home page or from the **Runtimes** menu in the
    header.

3.  In the sidebar, click **Traces**.
4.  Make sure youâre using the [business group](#business-groups) that contains the spans.
5.  Select up to the last seven days of spans (see [item 5](#traces-overview)).

    15 minutes is the default.

6.  Select the [environment](#environments) that contains your spans.
7.  Optionally, provide a [filter query](#filters) to narrow the search results.
8.  [Get trace details](#get-trace-details).

## Find Traces for Agent Visualizer Nodes

Agent Visualizer links to traces for agentic assets (agent, broker, and MCP server instances).

1.  In Agent Visualizer, click a node to open its detail card.
2.  Click **View Traces** to navigate to the traces for the node in **Trace Details** (in Anypoint
    Monitoring).
3.  [Get trace details](#get-trace-details).

For more information about nodes in Agent Visualizer, see
[Agent Visualizer Overview](../agent-visualizer/).

## Get Trace Details

After navigating to **Trace Details** from [**Traces Overview**](#trace-overview) in Anypoint
Monitoring or from a [node in Agent Visualizer](#visualizer), get information about the trace and
its spans.

1.  Review information about the trace:

    **Trace Details** displays the selected instanceâs traces in a span tree, starting from the root
    span for the instance.

    ![An instanceâs spans in the Traces Details page](_images/trace-details.png)
    1.  **Trace Details** page for a span selected from **Trace Overview** or through a node in
        Agent Visualizer
    2.  High-level data about the selected span:
        - **Status Code**: HTTP response status of the trace (for example, `0 No Response`,
          `200 OK`,`500 Internal Server Error`)
        - **Span Start Time**: The time the trace started, in UTC
        - **Total Response Time**: The time taken for the trace to execute
        - **Root Span**: The request or operation that initiated the trace

    3.  Search for spans by full or partial matches to the span name

        The page displays matching spans.

    4.  Trace hierarchy of spans, starting from the root span through its descendants
    5.  Trace ID of the root span

2.  To get more information about a span in the details page, click the span:

    ![Details of a span in the Traces Details page](_images/trace-details-span.png)
    1.  **Span Name**: Name of the span.
    2.  [Span details](#span-details)

## Filter Spans

To narrow the list of spans in **Traces Overview**, provide a filter query in the search field.

1.  Place your cursor within the search field and click to display a list of filters.
2.  Select a filter from the fieldâs dropdown.

    <table id="filter-list"><colgroup><col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Filter</th><th>Operators</th><th>Description</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><div><div><p><code>api_instance_id</code></p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p><code>=</code></p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Version of an API instance thatâs associated with a span. You can find the instanceâs ID in API Manager.</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><div><div><p><code>correlation_id</code></p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p><code>=</code></p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Correlation ID associated with a span (see <a href="../mule-runtime/latest/correlation-id">Correlation ID</a>).</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><div><div><p><code>entity_name</code></p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p><code>=</code>, <code>IN</code></p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Name of entities associated with spans.</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><div><div><p><code>http_status_code</code></p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p><code>=</code>, <code>IN</code></p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>HTTP status codes associated with span.</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><div><div><p><code>span_id</code></p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p><code>=</code></p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>ID of a span contained in traces.</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><div><div><p><code>span_name</code></p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p><code>=</code>, <code>IN</code></p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Name of a span.</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><div><div><p><code>trace_id</code></p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p><code>=</code>, <code>IN</code></p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>ID associated with a span.</p></div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

3.  Select an operator for the filter:
    - `=` accepts a single alphanumeric value.
    - `IN` accepts one or more comma-separated, alphanumeric values. The values get processed as
      logical ORs.

4.  Select a value from the search fieldâs **Top Value** list. If the value youâre looking for isnât
    in the list, start typing a partial match to your value, and then select the value.

    Tip: If youâre providing search values to the `IN` operator (such as
    `entity_name IN ['value1', 'value2']`), manually adding a comma after a value before selecting
    another value from the **Top Value** list can help with valid query formation.

    Example:

    This example gets spans named `my-span` that have status code '404', '502', or '200':

    `http_status_code IN [ '404', '502', '200' ] span_name = my-span`

To edit or delete a filter, click the filter, and then click **Edit Filter** or **Delete Filter**
from the **Actions** list for the filter. To avoid issues when editing, you can delete the filter
and then add it back with the values you want.

Tips:

- If no traces appear after applying a query, change your filters, the lookback setting, or business
  group.
- Selecting multiple filters of the same type isnât allowed.
- Filters get processed as logical ANDs. If a span doesnât match one of the filters, the query wonât
  return spans.

## Span Details

Traces provide information about a root span and its descendants in dedicated **Trace Details**
pages. Each span listed on the page links to details about that span:

- **Span Duration**: Time the span took to execute
- **Span Status**: Status of the span (**OK**, **ERROR**, **Unset**)
- **Span ID**: ID of the span
- **Span Kind**: Source of the span operation or request (**Server**, **Internal**, **Client**)
- **Span Start Time**: Time that the span started, in UTC
- **Trace ID**: ID of the trace in which the span occurred
- **Correlation ID**: Unique identifier thatâs generated before sending an event to the next
  processor in the Mule flow (see [Correlation ID](../mule-runtime/latest/correlation-id))
- **HTTP Status Code**: HTTP status code of a span that is a request. Statuses include **0 No
  Response**, **200 OK**, and **500 Internal Server Error**.
- **Entity Type**: Type of entity associated with the trace. Types include **App**, **API**.
- **Entity Name**: Name of the entity associated with the trace
- [**Business Group**](#business-groups) that the span belongs to
- [**Environment**](#environments) that the span belongs to
- **Parent Span ID**: ID of a span that contains the selected span

  Parent spans donât have a value in this field. Use the Trace ID to track this span.

- **View Logs**: Find the root span in Anypoint Monitoring logs.

## Environments

Select the environment containing the spans you want to review in **Traces Overview**.

Your organization administrator
[creates](../access-management/environments#to-create-a-new-environment) the environments that are
available to you.

Anypoint Platform supports these _types_ of
[environments](../access-management/environments#types-of-environments.adoc):

- **Design**
- **Production**
- **Sandbox**

## Business Groups

If you work from the root organization level, you can query all spans across the entire
organization. If youâre working from a business group thatâs a descendant of the root, the query
returns spans from that business group only.

To view or select a different business group, use the business group dropdown.

![Business group menu](_images/business-groups.png)

<table><tbody><tr><td><i data-value="1"></i><b>1</b></td><td>Business group that contains spans in the <strong>Span List</strong></td></tr><tr><td><i data-value="2"></i><b>2</b></td><td>Selectable business groups<div><p>In the example, the root organization is active.</p></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

## See Also

- [Exporting Telemetry Data to Third-Party Monitoring Systems](telemetry-exporter)
- [Enable Trace Data Collection](../cloudhub-2/ch2-deploy-shared-space#enable-trace-data-collection)
  (Shared Space)
- [Enable Trace Data Collection](../cloudhub-2/ch2-deploy-private-space#enable-trace-data-collection)
  (Private Space)
